strats.content:
# Border — Offices / Security Room (2F) · ATTACK

**Status:** ✅ Fully Developed
**Formation:** 3-2
**Developed:** 2026-03-14 (migrated from STACK_03)
**Site Context:** 2F east bomb site. Two hatches from Security Room to 1F Customs areas enable dual vertical pressure. Primary approach via Red Stairs (east), secondary via Long Hallway from Admin.

---

## Spawn

3-man group spawns Valley (central). Two solos spawn East (for vertical pressure setup). Adjust spawn split based on vertical vs. direct approach needs.

---

## Roles

| Player | Role | Operator | Group |
|--------|------|----------|-------|
| **Hound** | Anti-gadget | Anti-gadget | With Smigs (Ventilation approach) |
| **Sarge** | Map Control / Flex | Map Control / Flex | With Smigs and Hound (Ventilation approach) |
| **Smigs** | Hard Breach | Hard Breach | With Hound and Sarge (Ventilation approach) |
| **Peej** | Vert / Flex | Vert / Flex | With Grant (Upstairs approach) |
| **Grant** | Vert / Flex | Vert / Flex | With Peej (Upstairs approach) |

**Hound (Anti-gadget)** — Ventilation group with Smigs and Sarge. Push into Workshop, get bathroom wall for plant setup. Deny Kapkan/gadgets on approach.

**Sarge (Map Control/Flex)** — Ventilation group with Hound and Smigs. Push into Workshop, cut off flanks and rotations from site.

**Smigs (Hard Breach)** — Ventilation group with Hound and Sarge. Push into Workshop, establish entry for plant setup. Hard breach utility on critical walls.

**Peej (Vert/Flex)** — Upstairs with Grant. Establish crosses, eliminate or cut off roamers on 2F. Create space for Security Room push.

**Grant (Vert/Flex)** — Upstairs with Peej. Establish crosses, eliminate or cut off roamers. Vertical support for site execution.

---

## Execute Sequence

1. **Drone phase** — Ventilation team (Hound/Sarge/Smigs) drones Hallway and Workshop approaches. Upstairs team (Peej/Grant) drones Red Stairs, Long Hallway, and 2F roamer positions.

2. **Roamer pressure (Upstairs team)** — Peej and Grant move upstairs via Red Stairs approach. Establish crosses on 2F roamers. Eliminate or force rotations before main push.

3. **Bathroom push (Ventilation team)** — Hound, Sarge, and Smigs push through Bathroom toward Workshop. Destroy or clear gadgets on approach. Utility denial on key doorways.

4. **Workshop control** — Ventilation team establishes control of Workshop space. Identify bathroom wall for plant approach.

5. **Bathroom wall breach** — Smigs breaches bathroom wall, establishing secondary angle into site or pressure point.

6. **Security Room entry** — Upstairs team (Peej/Grant) pushes into Long Hallway and approaches Security Room from 2F side. Establish control of room.

7. **Coordinate push** — Ventilation team provides lower pressure (Bathroom/Workshop side). Upstairs team provides upper pressure (Long Hallway/Security side). Defenders squeezed from both directions.

8. **Site clear** — Establish full site control. Clear Offices and Security Room. Identify safe plant locations.

9. **Plant** — Plant in Lobby area or deep Workshop position depending on defender positions and remaining utility.

10. **Post-plant** — Team secures perimeter, holds cross angles, denies defuser plays.

---

## Plant Position (PP)

**Primary:** Lobby / Workshop area — central position with cover
**Alternate:** Bathroom extension if defenders are weighted toward Security side

---

## Post-Plant

| Player | Position | Job |
|--------|----------|-----|
| **Hound** | Bathroom side | Anti-gadget denial; watches Bathroom flank |
| **Sarge** | Site perimeter | Holds cross angles; denies rotations |
| **Smigs** | Site perimeter / corner | Holds secondary angles; supports defense |
| **Peej** | Long Hallway / 2F side | Holds upstairs denial; prevents rotation from 2F |
| **Grant** | Security Room / underneath | Holds defuser challenge angle; prevents late rotation |

---

## Watch For

- **Kapkan at Red Stairs:** Standard roamer trap placement. Upstairs team must drone and clear before committing. Loss of player to trap = loss of vertical advantage.

- **Roamer from Break Room / Admin area:** Secondary defender may push from 2F side during execute. Peej/Grant must establish crosses early before Ventilation team fully commits.

- **Aruni at Security Room entry:** Gate on Security Room doorway will damage team pushing through. Destroy or account for damage.

- **Valkyrie cameras on Red Stairs, Long Hallway, exterior windows:** Look for cams. Destroy early if spotted to deny intel to defenders.

- **Mute disruptors on 2F:** May prevent utility placement or gadget usage. Account for signal jamming.

- **Two hatches from Security Room:** Defense will likely have one operator managing both hatches for vertical denial. Expect roamer from below watching both hatch positions.

- **Bathroom ↔ Tellers wall extension:** Post-rework, Bathroom connects toward Tellers. Don't get flanked from unexpected Tellers-side pressure.

---

## Win Conditions

### Win A — Primary: Dual Pressure Push ✅
**When:** Roamer on 2F is eliminated/denied, Red Stairs is clear, Ventilation approach is open, Smigs utility is ready.

Upstairs team (Peej/Grant) establishes crosses and roamer denial. Ventilation team (Hound/Sarge/Smigs) pushes through Bathroom and Workshop. Site is squeezed from both directions. Plant executes safely.

**Who calls it:** Grant calls "Roamers cleared" from upstairs. Hound confirms Ventilation approach is clear. Sarge initiates coordinate push.

---

### Win B — Ventilation Pressure Only
**When:** Upstairs roamer is strong or well-positioned, Ventilation approach is more open than 2F approach.

Focus pressure on Ventilation/Bathroom side. Upstairs team (Peej/Grant) becomes supportive distraction rather than primary push. Ventilation team (Hound/Sarge/Smigs) executes site from below.

**Who calls it:** Sarge calls "Going Vent-first" early. Peej confirms roamer strong and backs off primary push role.

---

### Win C — Upstairs Chaos / Quick Roamer Win
**When:** Peej/Grant get early roamer kill(s), creating 5v3 or 5v4 early advantage.

Upstairs team pushes harder and faster. Early roamer elimination means Security Room is thin. Coordinate full push immediately.

**Who calls it:** Grant announces roamer kill; team accelerates primary execute.

---

### Win D — Bathroom Wall Denied
**When:** Smigs is dead, hard breach is banned, Bathroom wall has hardened defender (Castle, etc.).

**Adjustment:** Shift primary pressure to upstairs (Red Stairs/Long Hallway approach). Peej and Grant push harder into Security Room. Hound/Sarge hold lower pressure as support or secondary angle. Plant may go in Security Room instead of Workshop.

**Who calls it:** Smigs calls "Breach unavailable, going upstairs-primary."

---

## Audibles

- **Red Stairs locked down:** Don't force Upstairs team into dense trap/defender setup. Fall back to Ventilation-only pressure.
- **Ventilation approach too strong:** Pivot to Red Stairs/upstairs pressure. Abandon Bathroom approach if utility cost is too high.
- **Both upstairs operators down early:** Play 3v5 from Ventilation side. Don't push upstairs again — consolidate lower site pressure.
- **Smigs dies without breaching Bathroom:** Replan plant position. Bathroom breach may not be available — adjust to available angles.

---

## Formation Notes

The 3-2 formation creates asymmetric pressure: Ventilation team (3-man) applies sustained lower pressure, Upstairs team (2-man) applies tactical upper pressure. The key is coordination — if one vector becomes denied, the other team must pivot quickly. Flexibility in plant position (Workshop vs. Security vs. Bathroom area) keeps defenders guessing. Monitor both Bathroom wall and Red Stairs approach throughout — if either fully closes, call the audible immediately.

---

strats.content:
# Border — Armory Lockers / Archives (2F) · DEFENSE

**Status:** ✅ Fully Developed
**Developed:** 2026-03-14 (migrated from STACK_04)
**Note:** Strategy name: "Stair Denial"
⚠️ **Roster change 2026-03-21:** Peej (Maestro/Valkyrie) replaces Slug (Smoke/Valkyrie). Post-plant denial transitions from Smoke canisters to Maestro Evil Eye + LMG. Peej must remain near his Evil Eye to operate it. "Maestro" references throughout this file = the operator Maestro (Evil Eye + LMG), not Smoke canisters.

> **Site Context:** 2F north bomb site with Red and Yellow Stairs as primary attack vectors. Stairwell control is the backbone of this site — Grant's Kapkan traps at stair tops change the round before the team even fires a shot.

---

## Anchor Assignments

| Player | Operator | Position | Role |
|--------|----------|----------|------|
| **Hound** | Castle | Armory Lockers | Primary anchor — barricades Red Stairs entry, holds behind locker cover facing east entrance approach |
| **Peej** | Valkyrie / Maestro | Archives | Anchors deep in Archives/Offices area. Valkyrie cams on Yellow Stairs, Red Stairs, East Entrance exterior. Maestro post-plant denial on Archives plant. |
| **Smigs** | Aruni | Armory entry | Aruni gate on the Armory Lockers main entry from the Admin/Hall side |
| **Grant** | Kapkan / Thorn | Red Stairs and Yellow Stairs landings | Kapkan traps at the top of both stairwells. Thorn holds the 2F Hallway junction between Archives and Armory. |
| **Sarge** | Vigil | Roamer (East Entrance extended area or Break Room) | Gets early pressure, then returns through Admin toward 2F Hallway. Does NOT hard commit to stairs. |

**Hound (Castle)** — Armory Lockers. Barricades the Red Stairs entry into Armory and the Archives door from Administration side. Holds behind locker cover facing the East Entrance approach. Primary anchor — last to die if possible.

**Peej (Valkyrie / Maestro)** — Archives. If Valkyrie: places cams on Yellow Stairs, Red Stairs, and East Entrance exterior for intel. Roams slightly into Offices to place cams, then anchors deep in Archives post-placement. If Maestro: holds deep in Archives, reserves canister utility for post-plant denial on Archives plant spots.

**Smigs (Aruni)** — Armory entry. Aruni gate on the Armory Lockers main entry from the Admin/Hall side. Gate placement forces attackers to destroy it or take damage, slowing site entry. Holds adjacent to gate for gate-denial and cover.

**Grant (Kapkan / Thorn)** — Red Stairs and Yellow Stairs landings. Kapkan: place traps at the top of Red Stairs and the top of Yellow Stairs. These are mandatory placements — they change the outcome of multiple rounds by creating early damage/kills. If Thorn: hold the 2F Hallway junction between Archives and Armory, controlling the rotation hub.

**Sarge (Vigil)** — Roamer. East Entrance extended area or Break Room. Gets early pressure on approaching attackers, then returns through Admin toward the 2F Hallway junction. Does NOT hard commit to the stairwells themselves — goal is early pick or information, then disengage to rejoin anchor group.

---

## Gadget Deployment

- **Kapkan (Grant):** Top of Red Stairs (mandatory), top of Yellow Stairs (mandatory) — These are setup's backbone. Secondary Kapkan at East Entrance approach if time allows.
- **Aruni (Smigs):** Armory Lockers main entry arch — forces attackers to breach or tank damage
- **Valkyrie cams (Peej):** Yellow Stairs (looking down landing), Red Stairs (looking down landing), exterior East Vehicle Entrance — covers primary attack vectors and exterior rappel approaches
- **Maestro (Peej):** Reserve for Archives/Armory plant denial — pre-position at Archives doorway and any site-adjacent windows if plant is likely in Archives

---

## Reinforcement Priority

1. **Armory Lockers East Entrance wall (2F)** — primary external breach wall; highest priority defensive foundation
2. **Archives ↔ Administration wall** — divides objective zones; controls attacker rotation into site
3. **Archives ↔ East Hallway wall** — compartmentalizes Archives; prevents flanking from east side
4. **Armory ↔ Admin connecting wall** — controls internal circulation; prevents soft breaches into site

---

## Post-Plant Hold

| Player | Position | Job |
|--------|----------|-----|
| **Peej (Maestro)** | Archives depth | Canisters on Armory and Archives plant positions — two canister denial covers both bomb areas |
| **Hound** | Armory locker cover | Last anchor position; hard to shift from locker-side cover |
| **Smigs** | Armory/Archives connecting area | Defuser challenge point; blocks plant setup even after Aruni gate destruction |

---

## Win Conditions

### Win A — Primary: Stair Trap Setup ✅
**When:** Setup goes as planned — Kapkan traps placed at both stair tops, Aruni gate in place, Sarge gets early pressure.

The traps change the round before the team even fires a shot. If Grant gets 1–2 trap kills in the approach phase (Red/Yellow Stairs), the team plays a 5v3 or 5v4 post-plant. Peej's Maestro/Valkyrie intel combined with Hound's locker hold provides endgame control.

**Execution:** Defend stairwells passively pre-plant. Let Kapkan do the work. Once attackers commit to a stair (usually Yellow), the trap forces them to accept damage or retreat. This decision point is where attackers lose rounds.

---

### Win B — Sarge Roam Creates Man Advantage
**When:** Sarge gets one early pick on his roam (East Entrance or Break Room), creating 5v4.

The man advantage makes site defense extremely strong. Even if attackers continue their execute, team is playing a numbers game in defender favor. Sarge's job is to create this, then fall back to the anchor group.

**Execution:** Sarge must disengage after the pick. Do not chase for a second kill. If Sarge dies without a pick, the 5v4 goes the other direction — attackers have the advantage.

---

### Win C — Aruni Gate Denies Early Site Access
**When:** Attackers clear stairs but hit Aruni gate on main Armory entry, forcing them to choose: destroy gate (waste utility/time) or take damage.

Either choice delays the execute. If they destroy the gate, they've burned utility they need for plant setup. If they take damage, they enter site weakened.

**Execution:** Smigs holds near the gate for immediate counter-breach if destroyed. The gate is delay, not the final defense — support it with team presence.

---

### Win D — Roam Fails / Dies Early
**When:** Sarge takes a bad trade and dies in the first 30 seconds, or never gets the roam kill.

**Adjustment:** Do NOT chase Sarge's death or try to get revenge. Tighten the four anchors into site immediately. Hound, Peej, Smigs, Grant all fall back to core defensive positions. The Kapkan traps now become even more important — let them do the attrition work. Peej's Valkyrie cams provide the primary intel source since Sarge isn't roaming.

**Execution:** This is a pure stairwell defense now. Let attackers come to you. Trap damage + anchors = win condition.

---

### Win E — Traps All Cleared / No Early Picks
**When:** Attackers drone out and manually clear every Kapkan trap before committing to stairs.

**Adjustment:** This is a pure gunfight round. Hound's Castle barricades become the last line of delay instead of traps. Smigs' Aruni gate is the final attrition point. Peej should use Valkyrie cams this site if trap denial is expected — intel advantage compensates for the lost Kapkan kills.

**Execution:** Position for gun angles, not trap positioning. Hound's barricade now serves as a physical delay rather than a trap-based delay.

---

## Watch For

- **Heavy grenade utility on stairs:** Attackers may prenade before pushing stairs to set off Kapkan. If hearing lots of explosions on stairs approach, expect casualties to grenades rather than Kapkan.
- **Hibana / hard breach on Armory external wall:** Attackers may breach through exterior instead of stairs. Post-rework, exterior walls are more interior-covered, but still watch for this. Sarge's roam should catch this early.
- **Soft breach on non-stairwell walls:** If attackers decide to make new angles through soft walls instead of pushing stairs, Hound should anticipate this and hold angles accordingly. Reinforcement priority may need adjustment mid-round.
- **Yellow Stairs vs. Red Stairs choice:** Attackers will usually commit to one primary stair. Watch which one takes heavy fire early — that's your primary defense vector. Stack accordingly.
- **Nomad (Airjabs):** S-tier ban candidate. If Nomad not banned, expect Airjabs on stairwells to counter the trap setup. Nomad creates no-go zones on stairs, forcing attackers to find alternative routes.
- **Brava (Kludge Drone):** A-tier. Brava's drone can hack and reverse Smigs' Aruni gate. If Smigs hears the gate activate against the team, he must destroy it immediately.
- **Zero (Argus Cams):** S-tier. Argus Cams can be fired through the Armory walls to see the entire site and plant area. Peej should watch for green Argus Cams in the walls and destroy them immediately.

---

## Notes

This site lives and dies by stairwell control. Red and Yellow Stairs are the two attack vectors — Grant's Kapkan traps at both stair tops change the outcome of multiple rounds. Sarge's Break Room roam is high value — catch attackers pre-stair or get an exterior rappel pick, then fall back fast. Do NOT over-rotate Sarge into the stairwell itself; he's a roamer, not an anchor.

---

Holds Tellers from counter cover facing Main Entrance and Passport Check. Valkyrie cams on Main Entrance exterior, Red Stairs (above), and East exterior. Maestro for Customs and Tellers plant denial.
Aruni gate on the Passport Check arch into Customs
Kapkan traps in Passport Check hallway and Armory approach. Thorn watches Supply Room and Armory corridor.

roles.Role: Gets early pressure, returns through central 1F rotations. Exterior roamer catching attack setups before they reach site.

roles.Position: Canisters from Tellers counter through Customs Inspection and Main Entrance — covers both plant spots
Last anchor position; holds Customs counter angles

roles.Position: Defuser challenge from flank; watches doorway angles

strats.content:
# Border — Customs Inspection / Tellers (1F) · DEFENSE

**Status:** ✅ Fully Developed
**Developed:** 2026-03-14 (migrated from STACK_04)
**Note:** Strategy name: "East Hold"
⚠️ **Roster change 2026-03-21:** Peej (Maestro/Valkyrie) replaces Slug (Smoke/Valkyrie). Post-plant denial transitions from Smoke canisters to Maestro Evil Eye + LMG. Confirm Maestro placement angles for this site when next played. "Maestro" references throughout = Evil Eye + LMG, not Smoke canisters.

> **Site Context:** 1F east bomb site. Most exposed on Border — Main Entrance is a wide open approach. The Bathroom ↔ Tellers connection (Crimson Heist extension) is a blind spot that must be reinforced every round. Peej's Maestro post-plant from Tellers counter is the win condition.

---

## Anchor Assignments

| Player | Operator | Position | Role |
|--------|----------|----------|------|
| **Hound** | Castle | Customs Inspection (Customs Desk) | Barricades the Main Entrance exterior entry into Customs. Holds from counter cover facing main entrance approach. |
| **Peej** | Valkyrie / Maestro | Tellers | Holds Tellers from counter cover facing Main Entrance and Passport Check. Valkyrie cams on Main Entrance exterior, Red Stairs (above), and East exterior. Maestro for Customs and Tellers plant denial. |
| **Smigs** | Aruni | Customs entry / Passport Check | Aruni gate on the Passport Check arch into Customs |
| **Grant** | Kapkan / Thorn | Armory (1F) approach and Passport Check | Kapkan traps in Passport Check hallway and Armory approach. Thorn watches Supply Room and Armory corridor. |
| **Sarge** | Vigil | Roamer (East exterior / East Entrance area or 1F Hallway side) | Gets early pressure, returns through central 1F rotations. Exterior roamer catching attack setups before they reach site. |

**Hound (Castle)** — Customs Inspection (Customs Desk). Barricades the Main Entrance exterior entry into Customs. Holds from counter cover facing the main entrance approach. Primary anchor — controls the Customs side entry point.

**Peej (Valkyrie / Maestro)** — Tellers. If Valkyrie: place cams on Main Entrance exterior (watching south approach), East exterior side (watching flank), and Red Stairs landing above (watching vertical pressure). Roams slightly to place cams, then anchors deep in Tellers. If Maestro: holds deep in Tellers counter position, reserves canister utility for Customs and Tellers plant denial post-plant.

**Smigs (Aruni)** — Customs entry / Passport Check. Aruni gate on the Passport Check arch into Customs. Gate forces attackers to destroy it or tank damage entering the Customs area. Holds adjacent to gate.

**Grant (Kapkan / Thorn)** — Armory (1F) approach and Passport Check. Kapkan: place traps in Passport Check hallway (mandatory) and Armory access corridor (mandatory). These cover the two approach routes into the east objectives. Thorn: watches the Supply Room and Armory corridor, holding the rotation hub.

**Sarge (Vigil)** — Roamer. East exterior / East Entrance area or 1F Hallway side. Gets early exterior pressure before attackers reach site, or roams internal hallway side. Must have good escape route back to Passport Check / central area. Does NOT hard commit deep into 1F rotations.

---

## Gadget Deployment

- **Kapkan (Grant):** Passport Check hallway (mandatory), Armory access corridor (mandatory) — covers the two primary approach routes into the east objectives
- **Aruni (Smigs):** Passport Check main arch — forces attackers to destroy gate or accept damage entering Customs
- **Valkyrie cams (Peej):** Main Entrance exterior (south-facing approach), East exterior side (flank pressure), Passport Check hallway interior (from Customs side) — covers exterior pressure and interior approach
- **⚠️ Bathroom extension (post-rework):** Bathroom connects to Tellers from west side — attackers can breach from Bathroom extension into Tellers; watch for this every round and reinforce accordingly

---

## Reinforcement Priority

1. **Customs Inspection main entry wall — from Main Entrance into Customs** — seals the primary attacker entry; highest priority defensive foundation
2. **Tellers exterior wall — Main Entrance side window approach** — denies direct window breach into Tellers from exterior
3. **Passport Check remaining soft walls** (2 remain post-rework) — limits soft breaching options; identify and reinforce or watch carefully
4. **Bathroom ↔ Tellers wall (Crimson Heist extension) — MANDATORY EVERY ROUND** — prevents Bathroom flank into Tellers. This must be reinforced or watched every single round; attackers will exploit this if not defended.

---

## Post-Plant Hold

| Player | Position | Job |
|--------|----------|-----|
| **Peej (Maestro)** | Tellers / Customs area | Canisters from Tellers counter through Customs Inspection and Main Entrance — covers both plant spots |
| **Hound** | Customs depth counter cover | Last anchor position; holds Customs counter angles |
| **Smigs** | Passport Check remnant / Aruni gate cover | Defuser challenge from flank; watches doorway angles |

---

## Win Conditions

### Win A — Primary: Gate-Gated Counter Defense ✅
**When:** Setup goes as planned — Kapkan traps placed in Passport Check and Armory, Smigs' Aruni gate in place, Peej has canisters ready post-plant.

Smigs' gate at Passport Check forces attackers to destroy it or take damage. Grant's traps in the hallway create early damage/kills. Combined with Peej's Maestro post-plant from Tellers counter, the two-canister denial (Customs and Tellers) covers both plant spots. This is the primary win condition.

**Execution:** Hound holds Customs counter. Peej anchors Tellers. Smigs supports gate. Grant traps slow approach. Once plant goes down, Peej's canisters deny both Customs and Tellers plant spots simultaneously.

---

### Win B — Sarge Roam Gets Early Pick
**When:** Sarge gets one early pick on exterior or in 1F Hallway (catches attackers before they reach site approach), creating 5v4.

The man advantage makes site defense much easier. Early pick before site execute is high-value. Sarge must disengage immediately after the pick and return to anchor group.

**Execution:** Sarge gets the kill, then falls back to Passport Check area. Do NOT chase or over-commit to the roam.

---

### Win C — Kapkan Traps Deny Approach
**When:** Grant's Kapkan in Passport Check gets one or more kills; attackers take damage and may retreat or be forced to find alternative route.

Early trap kill(s) reduce attacker numbers before they reach site. Combined with Peej's post-plant Maestro, this creates strong defensive math.

**Execution:** Let the traps work. Position to support them. If Kapkan gets kills, site defense becomes much easier.

---

### Win D — Aruni Gate Denies Entry
**When:** Attackers clear Passport Check but hit Aruni gate, forcing them to destroy it or tank damage entering Customs.

The gate delays entry and forces utility expenditure. If destroyed, attackers have burned charges. If they tank damage, they enter weakened.

**Execution:** Smigs holds near gate for immediate counter-breach. Gate is delay, not final defense — pair it with team positioning.

---

### Win E — Sarge Roam Fails / Dies Early
**When:** Sarge gets no pick or dies in first 30 seconds without trading.

**Adjustment:** Do NOT chase Sarge's death. Tighten the four anchors into site. Hound, Peej, Smigs, Grant fall back to core defensive positions. Ignore the exterior roam and focus on site defense. Kapkan traps + Aruni gate become the primary defense. Peej's Valkyrie cams provide intel since Sarge isn't roaming.

**Execution:** Pure site defense. Let attackers come to you. Traps + gate + Maestro denial = win condition.

---

## Watch For

- **Bathroom extension flank (CRITICAL — every round):** Post-rework, Bathroom connects to Tellers as a soft wall/extension. Attackers can breach from Bathroom extension into Tellers. This is the #1 blind spot on this site. Reinforce or have a defender watching Bathroom-Tellers connection at ALL times. Loss of awareness here = loss of rounds.

- **Multi-angle Main Entrance pressure:** Attackers may press multiple angles on Main Entrance (windows, door, flanks) simultaneously. Sarge's roam should catch early commitment before they reach site. If Sarge dies, site pressure becomes extreme from multiple vectors.

- **Passport Check soft wall exploitation:** Post-rework, soft walls reduced from 4 to 2. Identify remaining soft walls and watch for soft breaches. If attacker creates new angle through soft wall, team must reposition accordingly.

- **Red Stairs vertical pressure (from above):** Red Stairs above can provide vertical pressure into Tellers area. Peej should watch for vertical attacks or destruction sounds from 2F above Tellers.

- **Supply Room flank:** Supply room relocated in rework — watch for attackers using Supply as flank route into site from Armory side. Grant's trap in Armory access corridor helps with this.

- **Window pressure from exterior:** Tellers has windows on Main Entrance side. Expect window pressure from exterior. Peej should be ready to deny this angle from Tellers counter.

- **Nomad (Airjabs):** S-tier ban candidate. If Nomad not banned, expect Airjabs on Main Entrance approach to counter external roamer freedom. Airjabs create no-go zones.

- **Zero (Argus Cams):** S-tier. Argus Cams can be fired through walls to see counter positions and plant area. Peej should watch for green Argus Cams in walls and destroy them.

- **Brava (Kludge Drone):** A-tier. Brava can hack and reverse Smigs' Aruni gate. If Smigs hears gate activate against the team, destroy it immediately.

---

## Notes

Customs/Tellers is the most exposed site on Border — Main Entrance is a wide open approach. The Bathroom ↔ Tellers connection (Crimson Heist) is a blind spot — Reinforcement Priority #4 (Bathroom ↔ Tellers wall) must happen every round without fail. Loss of awareness on Bathroom is a guaranteed round loss. Peej's Maestro post-plant from Tellers counter is the win condition: if the team holds to plant phase, canisters cover both plant spots. Grant's Passport Check traps slow the main approach. Sarge's exterior pressure catches attackers before they reach site. Execute this setup flawlessly and this site becomes manageable despite its exposed nature.

---

Gets one pressure kill, forces attacker to check Break Room, then disengages back to Admin ↔ Offices junction.

strats.content:
# Border — Offices / Security Room (2F) · DEFENSE

**Status:** ✅ Fully Developed
**Developed:** 2026-03-14 (migrated from STACK_04)
**Note:** Strategy name: "Hatch Stack"
⚠️ **Roster change 2026-03-21:** Peej (Maestro/Valkyrie) replaces Slug (Smoke/Valkyrie). Post-plant denial transitions from Smoke canisters dropped through hatches to Maestro Evil Eye cameras placed above the hatches — LMG fires downward into Customs. The core site mechanic (two hatches above Customs plant spots) still works with Maestro, but placement must be confirmed in-game. "Maestro denial" throughout this file = Evil Eye + LMG, not Smoke canisters.

> **Site Context:** 2F east bomb site with the most powerful Maestro setup on Border — two hatches from Security Room drop directly onto 1F Customs plant spots. This is the highest-value Maestro anchor position on the entire map.

---

## Anchor Assignments

| Player | Operator | Position | Role |
|--------|----------|----------|------|
| **Hound** | Castle | Offices | Barricades the main Office entry from Administration. Holds from desk/chair cover facing the Admin approach. |
| **Peej** | Valkyrie / Maestro | Security Room | Primary Security Room anchor. Valkyrie cams on both Security Room hatches and Red Stairs approach. Maestro holds for post-plant canister denial — two hatches make this the highest-value Maestro spot on Border. |
| **Smigs** | Aruni | Security Room entry | Aruni gate on the Security Room main door |
| **Grant** | Kapkan / Thorn | Red Stairs and East Entrance top | Kapkan traps at Red Stairs (east-side stairwell). Thorn watches the Admin ↔ Offices entry from Waiting Room side. |
| **Sarge** | Vigil | Roamer (Break Room or 2F East Hallway) | Gets one pressure kill, forces attacker to check Break Room, then disengages back to Admin ↔ Offices junction. |

**Hound (Castle)** — Offices. Barricades the main Office entry from Administration. Holds from desk/chair cover facing the Admin approach. Primary anchor — controls the Office side of the 2F objective.

**Peej (Valkyrie / Maestro)** — Security Room. If Valkyrie: place cams on both Security Room hatches (looking down into Customs below) and Red Stairs approach (looking up). Roams to place cams, then anchors deep in Security Room. If Maestro: holds deep in Security Room interior, reserves BOTH canister charges for post-plant denial. The two hatches make this the highest-value Maestro position on Border — two canisters drop straight down into Customs plant spots.

**Smigs (Aruni)** — Security Room entry. Aruni gate on the Security Room main door. Gate forces attackers to destroy it or tank damage entering the room. Holds adjacent to gate for support.

**Grant (Kapkan / Thorn)** — Red Stairs and East Entrance top. Kapkan: place trap at Red Stairs top (mandatory) and East Entrance threshold. Thorn: watches the Admin ↔ Offices entry from the Waiting Room side, holding the rotation junction.

**Sarge (Vigil)** — Roamer. Break Room or 2F East Hallway. Gets one early pressure kill (catches attackers checking Break Room or moving through hallway), forces the attacking team to alter approach, then DISENGAGES back to the Admin ↔ Offices junction. Does NOT hard commit to stairwell areas or roam too deep.

---

## Gadget Deployment

- **Kapkan (Grant):** Red Stairs top (mandatory), East Entrance threshold — covers the two primary stair approaches into this site
- **Aruni (Smigs):** Security Room door entry — forces attackers to destroy gate or accept damage entering the room
- **Valkyrie cams (Peej):** Both hatches in Security Room (looking down into Customs below), Red Stairs approach (looking up from stair landing) — covers vertical pressure and stair approach
- **Maestro (Peej):** Both Security Room hatches — two full canister denial covering both Customs plant spots. This is the defining feature of the site.

---

## Reinforcement Priority

1. **Security Room walls — all non-hatch walls** — establish the bomb site as a fortress; hatch floors are intentional vertical pressure vectors that work in defense's favor (Maestro canisters)
2. **Offices ↔ Admin entry wall** — divides objective zones; controls attacker rotation into Office side
3. **East Hallway wall adjacent to Offices** — compartmentalizes eastern circulation; prevents soft breaches into Offices
4. **Waiting Room ↔ Offices soft wall** — last-stand position for rotation flexibility; keep intact for late-round rotations

---

## Post-Plant Hold

| Player | Position | Job |
|--------|----------|-----|
| **Peej (Maestro)** | Security Room | Drops both hatch canisters on plant spot in Customs below — two canister denial is the win condition |
| **Hound** | Offices depth, desk cover | Last anchor position; holds cross angles on Offices side |
| **Smigs** | Security Room far corner | Defuser challenge point; covers the hatch interior angle |

---

## Win Conditions

### Win A — Primary: Hatch Stack Maestro Denial ✅
**When:** Setup goes as planned — Kapkan traps placed on stairs, Peej has both hatch canisters ready post-plant, attackers commit to plant in Customs below.

This is the defining win condition. Peej's two canisters drop through both Security Room hatches directly onto the plant spot in Customs below. Attackers plant, but can't defuse. Peej holds the canister denial, and the team secures the round.

**Execution:** Hound holds Office, Smigs covers Security Room far angle, Peej manages canister timing. The two hatches are the site's strength — use them to deny the plant, not to reinforce. Expect attacks from below (Customs pushing up through hatches), and use the canisters to deny that push.

---

### Win B — Sarge Roam Creates Early Advantage
**When:** Sarge gets one early pick (Break Room or hallway), creating 5v4 before the execute.

The man advantage makes site defense much easier. Even if attackers continue their execute, the 5v4 swing is significant. Sarge must disengage after the kill — do NOT chase or over-commit to the roam.

**Execution:** Sarge picks one kill, then falls back to Admin junction. Do not push further. Let Hound and Smigs hold site with the man advantage.

---

### Win C — Kapkan Traps Deny Stair Approach
**When:** Grant's Kapkan at Red Stairs top gets one or more kills; attackers take damage and retreat or are forced to find alternative route.

Early Kapkan kill(s) reduce attacker numbers before they reach site. Combined with the hatch advantage, this creates a strong 5v4 or 5v3 defense.

**Execution:** Let the traps work. Position defensively on stairs — if Kapkan gets kills, the math changes in defender favor immediately.

---

### Win D — Aruni Gate Denies Entry
**When:** Attackers clear stairs but hit Aruni gate on Security Room entry, forcing them to destroy it or take damage entering.

The gate delays entry and forces utility expenditure. If destroyed, attackers have burned charges. If they tank damage, they enter weakened.

**Execution:** Smigs holds near gate for immediate counter-breach. Don't over-commit to the gate defense — it's a delay tool, not the final defense.

---

### Win E — Sarge Roam Fails / Dies Early
**When:** Sarge gets no pick or dies in first 30 seconds without trading.

**Adjustment:** Do NOT chase Sarge's death. Tighten the four anchors into site. Peej, Hound, Smigs, Grant fall back to core defensive positions. Ignore the roam side and focus on site defense. Kapkan traps + hatch denial become the primary defense. Peej's Valkyrie cams provide primary intel since Sarge isn't roaming.

**Execution:** Pure site defense. Let attackers come to you. Traps + hatch canisters = win condition.

---

## Watch For

- **Dual hatch destruction (attackers prioritize both hatches):** Attackers may send utility to destroy both hatches before pushing site. If both hatches are open, Maestro denial becomes impossible. Watch for coordinated hatch destruction and defend accordingly.

- **Vertical pressure from 1F Customs:** Attackers will push UP through hatches from below, trying to breach Security Room from underneath. Peej must watch both hatch holes and be ready to canister them before attackers establish position.

- **Bypassing Red Stairs (soft breach alternative):** Attackers may find soft wall alternatives to Red Stairs. Hound should listen for soft breach sounds and reinforce alternatives if available mid-round.

- **Kapkan trap manual clear:** Attackers may send a drone or operator to manually clear Red Stairs Kapkan before pushing. If you hear grenades or see operators clearing, expect the stair push without trap damage.

- **Admin rotation push:** Attackers may push through Admin toward Offices instead of using stairs. Hound should be ready for Admin-side entry, not just Red Stairs approach.

- **Nomad (Airjabs):** S-tier ban candidate. If Nomad not banned, expect Airjabs on Red Stairs to counter Kapkan. Nomad creates no-go zones, forcing alternative routes.

- **Zero (Argus Cams):** S-tier. Argus Cams can breach Security Room walls and watch the hatches, seeing Peej pre-position for post-plant. Peej should check for green cams in walls and destroy them before committing to hatch position.

- **Brava (Kludge Drone):** A-tier. Brava can hack and reverse Smigs' Aruni gate. If Smigs hears the gate activate against the team, destroy it immediately before it becomes a liability.

---

## Notes

Offices/Security Room has the most powerful Maestro setup on the entire map — two hatches that drop directly onto 1F Customs plant spots. Peej should NEVER leave Security Room past the 60-second mark. Grant's traps slow the stair approach, Sarge buys time in Break Room with early pressure, Peej controls the endgame from Security Room with hatch denial. This is a high-value site to hold if the setup is properly executed.

---

strats.content:
# Border — Workshop / Ventilation (1F) · DEFENSE

**Status:** ✅ Fully Developed
**Developed:** 2026-03-14 (migrated from STACK_04)
**Note:** Strategy name: "West Side Hold"
⚠️ **Roster change 2026-03-21:** Peej (Maestro/Valkyrie) replaces Slug (Smoke/Valkyrie). Post-plant denial transitions from Smoke canisters to Maestro Evil Eye + LMG. "Maestro" references throughout = Evil Eye + LMG, not Smoke canisters. Confirm Evil Eye placement in Vent doorway for Workshop denial when next played.

> **Site Context:** 1F west bomb site with industrial machinery layout. Tight enclosed spaces create close-quarters angles. Grant's roamer setup here is the best on the map — Main Lobby is a natural Vigil hunting ground.

---

## Anchor Assignments

| Player | Operator | Position | Role |
|--------|----------|----------|------|
| **Hound** | Castle / Frost | Workshop | Barricades the main Workshop entry from Main Lobby. Holds from machinery cover. If Frost: beartraps at Ventilation entry and Workshop stair base. |
| **Peej** | Valkyrie / Maestro | Ventilation Room | Holds Vent from the machinery corner. Valkyrie cams on Yellow Stairs (above), Valley Spawn exterior, and Main Lobby center. Maestro holds for Workshop plant. |
| **Smigs** | Aruni | Workshop / Vent connecting entry | Aruni gate on the direct Workshop ↔ Vent connection |
| **Grant** | Kapkan / Thorn | Yellow Stairs bottom and Main Lobby approach | Kapkan at Yellow Stairs bottom and Main Lobby chokepoint. Thorn watches 1F Hallway from Main Entrance. |
| **Sarge** | Vigil | Roamer (Main Lobby or Armory 1F) | This is Sarge's best roamer site — Main Lobby is a natural Vigil hunting ground. Gets early pressure, then returns to anchor group. |

**Hound (Castle / Frost)** — Workshop. Barricades the main Workshop entry from Main Lobby. Holds from machinery cover facing the lobby approach. If Frost: place beartraps at the Ventilation entry (inside Workshop side) and at the base of Yellow Stairs (internal approach) to catch early rotations.

**Peej (Valkyrie / Maestro)** — Ventilation Room. If Valkyrie: place cams on Yellow Stairs (interior, ground-up view), Main Lobby center (watching entry pressure), and Valley Spawn exterior. Roams to place cams, then anchors deep in Ventilation from machinery corner. If Maestro: holds deep in Ventilation interior, reserves canister utility for Workshop plant denial.

**Smigs (Aruni)** — Workshop/Vent connecting entry. Aruni gate on the direct Workshop ↔ Vent connection. Gate forces attackers to destroy it or tank damage moving between the two bomb rooms. Holds adjacent to gate.

**Grant (Kapkan / Thorn)** — Yellow Stairs bottom and Main Lobby approach. Kapkan: place at Yellow Stairs bottom (mandatory) and Main Lobby entrance arch/chokepoint. Thorn: watches 1F Hallway from Main Entrance side, holding the circulation junction.

**Sarge (Vigil)** — Roamer. Main Lobby or Armory (1F). This is Sarge's best roamer site — Main Lobby is a natural Vigil hunting ground with tight corners, multiple angles, and defenders can see attackers committing from cams before Sarge commits to the fight. Gets early pressure, then returns through Main Lobby rotation toward Yellow Stairs area.

---

## Gadget Deployment

- **Kapkan (Grant):** Yellow Stairs bottom (mandatory), Main Lobby entrance arch — covers primary attacker approach vectors into Workshop/Ventilation
- **Aruni (Smigs):** Workshop↔Vent connecting entry — forces attackers to destroy gate or accept damage moving between bomb rooms
- **Valkyrie cams (Peej):** Yellow Stairs interior (ground up view), Main Lobby center, Valley Spawn exterior — covers vertical approach and exterior pressure points
- **Frost beartraps (Hound alt):** Base of Yellow Stairs (internal), Ventilation machine cover area (catching rotations between bomb rooms)

---

## Reinforcement Priority

1. **Workshop ↔ Main Lobby wall — primary** — seals most direct breach into Workshop from attacker entry point; highest priority defensive foundation
2. **Workshop ↔ Armory wall** — divides west and center 1F; prevents flanking rotations
3. **Ventilation exterior wall — prevents Valley-spawn side breach** — seals exterior pressure from Valley side
4. **Yellow Stairs ceiling/floor wall (if applicable)** — controls vertical access from 2F; soft walls should remain destructible to enable vertical pressure denial

---

## Post-Plant Hold

| Player | Position | Job |
|--------|----------|-----|
| **Peej (Maestro)** | Vent area | Workshop plant denial from Vent doorway — canisters block workshop plant access and plant completion |
| **Hound** | Workshop depth, machinery cover | Last anchor position; holds machinery angles on Workshop side |
| **Smigs** | Workshop/Vent junction | Defuser challenge from Aruni gate remnant position; watches junction angles |

---

## Win Conditions

### Win A — Primary: Yellow Stairs Trap Setup + Lobby Control ✅
**When:** Setup goes as planned — Kapkan trap at Yellow Stairs bottom, Grant holds Main Lobby roamer position early, Sarge gets early pressure from roam.

Grant's Yellow Stairs trap at the bottom is the setup's backbone. If attackers push down Yellow Stairs from 2F, they hit the trap. If Sarge gets early roam kill in Main Lobby, the 5v4 makes site defense extremely strong. Combined with Hound's Workshop hold and Smigs' gate, the defense becomes very solid.

**Execution:** Grant holds the trap and Main Lobby. Sarge pressures hard early. Hound holds Workshop depth. Let the trap and roamer do the early work.

---

### Win B — Sarge Roam Creates Early Advantage
**When:** Sarge gets one early pick in Main Lobby (his best roamer site on Border), creating 5v4.

Main Lobby is a tight-corner hunting ground for Vigil. Sarge can see attackers committing before they see him. One pick here is high-value. The 5v4 advantage makes site defense much easier.

**Execution:** Sarge gets the pick, then disengages back to Yellow Stairs junction. Do NOT chase for a second kill or stay in Main Lobby — fall back to anchor group.

---

### Win C — Aruni Gate Denies Internal Movement
**When:** Attackers reach site but hit Aruni gate on Workshop ↔ Vent connection, forcing them to choose: destroy gate (waste utility) or take damage moving between sites.

The gate delays internal site coordination. If destroyed, utility is spent. If they tank damage, they enter one or both rooms weakened.

**Execution:** Smigs holds near gate for immediate counter-breach. The gate is delay, not the final defense — pair it with team positioning.

---

### Win D — Kapkan Trap Denies Stair Entry
**When:** Grant's Kapkan at Yellow Stairs bottom gets a kill or forces attackers to take damage and retreat.

Early Kapkan kill reduces attacker numbers before they reach site. Combined with the soft site layout, this creates strong defensive math.

**Execution:** Position to support the trap. If Kapkan gets a kill, the attacker pressure immediately decreases.

---

### Win E — Roam Fails / Sarge Dies Early
**When:** Sarge gets no pick or dies in first 30 seconds without trading.

**Adjustment:** Do NOT chase Sarge's death. Tighten the four anchors into site. Hound, Peej, Smigs, Grant all fall back to core defensive positions. Ignore the Main Lobby roam and focus on site defense. Kapkan trap + Aruni gate become the primary defense. Peej's Valkyrie cams provide intel since Sarge isn't roaming.

**Execution:** Pure site defense. Let attackers come to you. Trap + gate + Maestro denial = win condition.

---

## Watch For

- **Multi-entry pressure (Main Lobby + Yellow Stairs + Ventilation exterior):** Workshop/Ventilation can be attacked from three directions. Sarge may get overwhelmed if attackers split pressure. Watch for coordinated multi-angle attacks.

- **Armory 1F flank:** Attackers may push Armory side to flank Workshop. Grant should be aware of this rotation and hold accordingly. Hound's Castle barricade on Workshop entry limits this, but watch for soft breach alternatives.

- **Yellow Stairs vertical from above:** 2F above has soft floors. Attackers may destroy floor and create new angles into Workshop/Ventilation from above. Listen for destruction sounds on 2F.

- **Kapkan trap manual clear:** Attackers may send drone/operator to manually clear Yellow Stairs Kapkan before pushing. If hearing grenades or destruction, expect stair push without trap damage.

- **Bathroom extension (post-rework):** Bathroom connects toward Tellers area and may allow unexpected flank pressure from east side. Don't get caught off-guard by Bathroom-side attacks on the site.

- **Nomad (Airjabs):** S-tier ban candidate. If Nomad not banned, expect Airjabs on Yellow Stairs or Main Lobby to counter Kapkan and roamer freedom. Airjabs create no-go zones.

- **Brava (Kludge Drone):** A-tier. Brava can hack and reverse Smigs' Aruni gate into a liability. If Smigs hears gate activate against the team, destroy it immediately.

- **Lesion (Gu mines):** A-tier. Lesion mines seeded through Main Lobby and Yellow Stairs area ping defenders when triggered, giving away positions. Move deliberately through approach areas.

---

## Notes

Grant's Yellow Stairs trap at the bottom is the setup's backbone. Sarge has maximum freedom to roam here — Main Lobby is the perfect Vigil hunting ground. Tight corners, multiple angles, defenders can see attackers committing from Valkyrie cams before Sarge commits to the fight. If the roam gets early kills, the rest of the defense becomes very strong. If the roam dies without value, tighten into site and let the trap/gate do the work.

---

overviewContent:
# Border — Overview

**Environment:** Middle East military border checkpoint facility; sand/interior mix with open air pathways
**Floors:** 2 (1F, 2F) + Exterior rooftops and catwalks
**In Y11S1 Ranked Pool:** ✅ Yes — first half of season only
**Year Added / Reworked:** Added Y2S1 (2016); major rework in Y5S3 (Crimson Heist, 2020); Y11S1 mid-season updates

> 📌 **Y11S1 Note:** Border is in the first-half Y11S1 ranked pool. The stack's exceptional Border performance (Sarge 76.9%, all players 57%+) makes this a high-priority protect/keep map early in the season before mid-season pool swap.

---

## Overview

Border is a two-story Middle East military checkpoint facility with emphasis on exterior access and multi-directional attack routes. The map is highly destructible with open air pathways creating constant pressure from multiple directions. Border's defining characteristic is openness and multiple exterior entry points (East Vehicle Entrance, Valley, West Vehicle Exit) enabling coordinated multi-angle attacks. The map features prominent vertical stairwell access (Yellow Stairs central, Red Stairs east) and 2F Security Room hatches providing dual vertical pressure vectors into 1F Customs areas. The Crimson Heist rework (Y5S3) introduced layout changes including 1F Bathroom extension, Supply Room relocation, reduced soft walls in Passport Check area, 2F Armory Lockers redesign, and extended 2F Archives/Offices configuration. Border is the stack's strongest map with dominant win rates across all players (57%-76.9%) and deep strategic understanding of site control and exterior pressure.

---

## Bomb Sites

### Site 1 — Armory Lockers / Archives — 2F
**Rooms:** Armory Lockers (1A) / Archives (1B)
**Common Attack Approach:** Yellow Stairs primary approach from Main Lobby; exterior catwalks secondary; exterior window pressure
**Common Defense:** Stair denial with Kapkan at Yellow Stairs top; Aruni gate at north site entries; roamer pressure from Break Room area
**Key Feature:** North 2F site with Red/Yellow Stairs as approach vectors. Soft floor above Main Lobby enables vertical pressure from below.
**Attack Strategy:** See ATK_Armory_Archives.md
**Defense Strategy:** See DEF_Armory_Archives.md

### Site 2 — Offices / Security Room — 2F
**Rooms:** Offices (2A) / Security Room (2B)
**Common Attack Approach:** Red Stairs primary; East Entrance secondary; dual hatch destruction from above creates vertical splits
**Common Defense:** Anchor holds Security Room with deep position; roamer from Admin watches Long Hallway approaches; hatch monitors for vertical pressure
**Key Feature:** TWO hatches from Security Room down to 1F Customs Inspection AND Customs Desk — enables simultaneous vertical pressure from 2F to 1F
**Attack Strategy:** See ATK_Offices_Security.md
**Defense Strategy:** See DEF_Offices_Security.md

### Site 3 — Workshop / Ventilation — 1F
**Rooms:** Workshop (3A) / Ventilation (3B)
**Common Attack Approach:** Main Lobby entry (west side); Yellow Stairs from north; exterior windows
**Common Defense:** Anchor holds Workshop machinery cover; Ventilation as secondary position or roamer trap; vertical defenders manage 2F hatch pressure
**Key Feature:** West 1F industrial site. Yellow Stairs provides north approach. Soft floor destructibility creates vertical pressure opportunity from 2F above.
**Attack Strategy:** See ATK_Workshop_Ventilation.md (⚠️ Partially Developed)
**Defense Strategy:** See DEF_Workshop_Ventilation.md

### Site 4 — Customs Inspection / Tellers — 1F
**Rooms:** Customs Inspection (4A) / Tellers (4B)
**Common Attack Approach:** Main Entrance primary (south); Red Stairs from north; Bathroom flank (post-rework)
**Common Defense:** Anchor holds Customs counter; Tellers secondary; roamer from Passport Check/Armory intercepts; Bathroom extension creates additional anchor option
**Key Feature:** East 1F administrative site. Red Stairs and Security Room hatches above provide vertical pressure vectors. Bathroom extension (post-rework) connects from west.
**Attack Strategy:** See ATK_Customs_Tellers.md (❌ Not Developed)
**Defense Strategy:** See DEF_Customs_Tellers.md

---

## Room Names & Community Callouts

### 2F (Top Floor) — Verified names
| Room | Callout | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| Armory Lockers | Armory / Lockers | Bomb site (1A); locker storage area |
| Archives | Archives / Archive | Bomb site (1B); extended into Offices area |
| Offices | Office / Offices | Bomb site (2A); administrative spaces |
| Security Room | Security / Sec | Bomb site (2B); TWO hatches to 1F Customs |
| Small Hall | Small Hall | North connecting passage |
| Break Room | Break | Utility staging area; balcony access (post-rework) |
| Fountain | Fountain | Central connecting area |
| Admin | Admin / Administration | Central rotation hub |
| Long Hallway | Long Hall / Hallway | Main 2F east-west passage |
| CCTV | CCTV | Monitoring room; northwest-central |
| East Entrance | East / East Ent | Stairwell access; east side |
| Red Stairs | Red / Red Stairs | East-side vertical staircase |
| Armory Hallway | Armory Hall | Corridor connecting north site |
| Waiting Room | Waiting | East-center room circulation |

### 1F (Ground Floor) — Verified names
| Room | Callout | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| Workshop | Workshop / Work | Bomb site (3A); industrial space (west) |
| Ventilation | Vent / Ventilation | Bomb site (3B); HVAC machinery room |
| Customs Inspection | Customs / Customs Desk | Bomb site (4A); administrative counter |
| Tellers | Tellers / Teller | Bomb site (4B); counter area with windows |
| Main Lobby | Main / Lobby | Central circulation hub; attacker primary entry |
| Supply | Supply | Storage area (relocated in rework) |
| Armory | Armory / Arm | Weapons storage; rotation hub |
| Passport Check | Passport / Passport Check | Hallway connecting center to east |
| Red Stairs | Red / Red Stairs | East-side vertical staircase |
| East Entrance | East / East Ent | Stairwell access; east side |
| Bathroom (1F) | Bathroom / Bath | Extended to Tellers (post-rework); west-east connection |
| Yellow Stairs | Yellow / Stairs | Central staircase; main vertical axis |
| Hallway (1F) | Hallway / Hall | Main 1F passage connecting areas |
| Main Entrance | Entrance / Main Entrance | Interior circulation near south exterior entry |

### Exterior & Spawns
| Location | Callout | Notes |
|----------|---------|-------|
| East Vehicle Entrance | East | Northeast attacker spawn |
| Valley | Valley / Mid | Center attacker spawn |
| West Vehicle Exit | West | Northwest attacker spawn |
| Catwalks / Rooftops | Catwalks / Roof | Elevated external walkways; rappel routes |
| Main Entrance (exterior) | Front / Front Gate | South-facing main exterior entry |
| East exterior side | East Side / East Ext | Right-side building exterior |
| West exterior side | West Side / West Ext | Left-side building exterior |

---

## Key Walls & Hatches

### Critical Reinforced Walls
- **Armory Lockers entries (2F):** Multiple walls segmenting north site from central areas
- **Archives ↔ Offices (2F):** Divides bomb objectives; primary reinforcement
- **Security Room walls (2F):** Primary site protection; hatch floors remain critical
- **Red Stairs landings (both floors):** Vertical access control points
- **Customs Inspection entry (1F):** Funnel attacker pressure through controlled entry
- **Workshop/Ventilation walls (1F):** West site compartmentalization

### Key Destructible/Soft Walls
- **Yellow Stairs floor/ceiling:** Central vertical destructible passage between 1F and 2F (CRITICAL)
- **2F floors over 1F:** Multiple destructible ceiling points enable vertical pressure
- **Passport Check area:** Soft walls reduced from 4 to 2 in Crimson Heist; identify remaining breach opportunities
- **Bathroom ↔ Tellers wall (1F):** Extended bathroom creates new soft connection (post-rework)
- **Main Lobby walls:** Soft destruction opportunities creating rotation access

### Key Hatches (5 total)
- **Security Room ↔ Customs Inspection (2F→1F):** Vertical pressure from Security Room above into Customs desk area
- **Security Room ↔ Customs Desk (2F→1F):** Second hatch from Security Room to ground Customs
- **Yellow Stairs vertical:** Destructible floor between 1F Main Lobby and 2F center
- **Red Stairs vertical:** Destructible passage between 1F Tellers and 2F east areas
- **Additional vertical connections:** Between floors for roamer access and pressure

---

## Stack Performance Notes (Y10S4)

| Player | Operator | Win Rate | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Sarge | — | 76.9% | Border specialist; exceptional performance |
| Hound | — | 60.7% | Dominant performance |
| Smigs | — | 58.1% | Strong consistent results |
| Grant | — | 57.6% | Solid fundamentals |
| Peej | — | _TBD_ | New to main stack 2026-03-21; data pending |
| Slug | — | 57.5% | Strong positive performance *(B Team as of 2026-03-21; historical data)* |

**Notes:** Border is the stack's BEST map — Sarge leads at 76.9%, all players maintain 57%+ win rate. The team has developed 2 full attack strategies with deep site understanding and strong roamer coordination. Exceptional across-the-board performance correlates with effective exterior pressure execution and excellent stairwell control. No defensive weaknesses identified. Consider Border a high-confidence map for draft/selection. Recommend priority protection during Y11S1 pool availability.

---

_Confidence: High — Border is a well-established competitive map with standardized callout names. Core callouts verified (Armory, Archives, Security Room, Customs, Tellers, Main Lobby, Yellow Stairs, Red Stairs) verified via map images and community standard. Crimson Heist rework changes documented: 1F Bathroom extension, Supply Room relocation, Passport Check soft wall reduction (4→2), 2F Armory layout redesign, Archives extension, 2F hatches to 1F Customs areas confirmed. Y11S1 rework status should be spot-checked in-game for any mid-season updates not reflected in floor plans._
referenceContent:
# Border — Map Reference

**Environment:** Middle East military border checkpoint facility; sand/interior mix with open air pathways
**Floors:** 2 (1F, 2F) + Exterior rooftops and catwalks
**In Y10S4 Ranked Pool:** Yes
**Year Added / Reworked:** Y2S1 (Dust Line, 2016); major rework in Y5S3 (Crimson Heist, 2020)

---

## Overview

Border is a two-story Middle East military checkpoint facility with emphasis on exterior access and multi-directional attack routes. The map is highly destructible with open air pathways creating constant pressure from multiple directions. Border's defining characteristic is openness and multiple exterior entry points (East Vehicle Entrance, Valley, West Vehicle Exit) enabling coordinated multi-angle attacks. The map features prominent vertical stairwell access (2F Security Room hatches to 1F Customs areas) and wide administrative spaces. Crimson Heist rework (Y5S3) made layout changes including 1F Bathroom extension, 1F Supply Room relocation, reduced soft walls in Passport Check area, 2F Armory Lockers layout redesign, and extended 2F Archives/Offices configuration. Border is considered the stack's strongest map with nearly universal high win rates (57%+) and Sarge leading at 76.9% win rate. The team has developed 2 full strategies with deep understanding of site control and exterior pressure.

---

## Bomb Sites

### Site: Armory Lockers / Archives — 2F
**Rooms:** Armory Lockers (bomb site A) / Archives (bomb site B)
**Description:** 2F north bomb site with Armory Lockers (storage area with lockers) and Archives (extended into Offices per Crimson Heist rework). Armory Lockers has layout similar to Road to Six Invitational 2021 Stadium; floor covering external walls made solid post-rework, reducing vulnerability to exterior breaches. Archives extended into Offices with East Entrance extended to cover external wall areas; now fully interior. Multiple windows provide exterior pressure points. Red Stairs and Yellow Stairs both connect to this site.
**Common defense:** Anchor holds Armory/Archives with cover behind storage/furniture. East Entrance roamer monitors stairs. Deployment of shields in key corridors controls entry routes from stairs. Vertical roamer monitors upper floor for exterior repel attacks.
**Attack approach:** Clear stairs areas (Red/Yellow) before executing onto site. Destroy windows for direct site entry from exterior. Utility denial in stairwell and East Entrance creates space for site execution. Coordinated utility setup before pushing Armory.

### Site: Offices / Security Room — 2F
**Rooms:** Offices (bomb site A) / Security Room (bomb site B)
**Description:** 2F east/southeast bomb site featuring Offices (administrative spaces) and Security Room (secured area). Security Room has 2 hatches: to 1F Customs Inspection and to 1F Customs Desk, creating multiple vertical pressure vectors. Offices space provides moderate cover with furniture. Site is accessible from Administration, East Stairs, and upper circulation areas.
**Common defense:** Anchor holds Office desk positions with cover. Security Room anchor holds room interior. Roamers monitor East/Red Stairs and Administration approaches. Hatches are critical monitoring points; vertical defenders manage hatch zones from below.
**Attack approach:** Control East Stairs/Red Stairs first. Destroy Security Room hatches to split vertical pressure and ground coordination. Push through Administration to Office. Execute with utility denial on stairwell routes. Security Room clear before Office final push.

### Site: Workshop / Ventilation Room — 1F
**Rooms:** Workshop (bomb site A) / Ventilation Room (bomb site B)
**Description:** 1F west bomb site with Workshop (larger industrial space) and Ventilation/HVAC room (tight machinery-filled space). Site vulnerable to vertical pressure from 2F hatches. Industrial layout offers furniture/machinery cover but challenging angles. Bathroom/exterior walls extended post-Crimson Heist to improve site connectivity. Main Lobby provides rotation route.
**Common defense:** Anchor holds Workshop with equipment/machinery cover. Ventilation as secondary or trap position. Vertical roamers defend from upper floor. Utility (shields) positioned to deny easy site entry. Limited traditional anchor positions due to industrial nature.
**Attack approach:** Clear Main Lobby and control external/interior approaches. Vertical pressure from 2F hatches splits defenders. Breach Ventilation and Workshop with coordinated utility denial. Establish Workshop control before Ventilation final push.

### Site: Customs Inspection / Tellers — 1F
**Rooms:** Customs Inspection (bomb site A) / Tellers (bomb site B)
**Description:** 1F east bomb site with Customs Inspection (open administrative area, also called Customs Desk) and Tellers (administrative counter area). Post-Crimson Heist, 1F Bathroom extended to cover exterior walls of 1F Tellers as connecting wall between sites; reduced soft walls in Passport Check area from four to two. Supply Room relocated in rework. Site connects to Main Entrance, Armory, and Passport Check areas providing multiple rotation routes.
**Common defense:** Anchor holds Customs Inspection counter position with furniture cover. Tellers anchor holds from window counter angles. Roamers from Passport Check and Armory intercept early attacks. Multiple defensive rotation options available. Bathroom extension post-rework creates additional anchor positioning.
**Attack approach:** Push from Main Entrance into Customs Inspection. Bathroom connection enables flanking into Tellers. Supply Room clear for flank denial. Soft wall destruction in Passport Check reduces from four to two walls. Utility covers main entry corridor. Coordinated two-point push executes both Customs and Tellers.

---

## Room Names & Community Callouts

### Attacker Spawns (3 total per wiki)
| Location | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| East Vehicle Entrance | "East" | Northeast spawn area |
| Valley | "Valley / Mid" | Middle spawn area |
| West Vehicle Exit | "West" | Northwest spawn area |

### Roof / Exterior Catwalks
| Location | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Northeast catwalks | "Catwalks / Roof" | Elevated external rooftop walkways |
| Break Room balcony | "Balcony / Break Balcony" | Upper balcony structure (post-rework addition) |
| East Stairs exterior area | "East Roof" | External area near East Stairs |
| Exterior walls | "Outside / Exterior" | General exterior pressure routes |

### 2F (Second Floor)
| Room / Location | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Armory Lockers | "Armory / Lockers" | Bomb site A; locker storage area (redesigned in Crimson Heist) |
| Archives | "Archives / Archive" | Bomb objective; extended into Offices post-rework |
| Offices | "Office / Offices" | Bomb site; extended Archives area |
| Security Room | "Security / Sec Room" | Bomb site B; secured interior space with 2 hatches to 1F |
| East Entrance | "East / East Entrance" | Stairwell access; extended to cover external walls (post-rework) |
| East Stairs | "East Stairs / Stairs" | Vertical connection on east side |
| Red Stairs | "Red Stairs / Red" | East-side stairwell vertical |
| Yellow Stairs | "Yellow Stairs / Yellow / Stairs" | Central/west vertical stairwell |
| Break Room | "Break / Break Room" | Small utility staging room |
| Administration / Admin | "Admin" | Administrative office area |
| Waiting Room | "Waiting" | Room area (connects to Break Room balcony post-rework) |
| 2F Main Hallway | "2F Hall / Hallway" | Central passage on second floor |
| East Hallway | "East Hall / Hallway" | East-side corridor |

### 1F (First Floor)
| Room / Location | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Workshop | "Workshop / Work" | Bomb site A; large industrial space (west side) |
| Ventilation Room | "Vent / Ventilation" | Bomb site B; HVAC machinery room (west side) |
| Customs Inspection | "Customs / Customs Desk" | Bomb site A (east side); administrative counter area |
| Customs Desk | "Customs Desk / Customs" | Counter area; Customs Inspection zone |
| Tellers | "Tellers / Teller" | Bomb site B; counter area with windows |
| Supply Room | "Supply / Supply Room" | Storage area (relocated in Crimson Heist rework) |
| Armory | "Armory / Arm" | Weapons storage; defender rotation point |
| Passport Check | "Passport / Passport Check" | Hallway area (soft walls reduced from 4 to 2 in rework) |
| Main Lobby | "Main / Main Lobby" | Primary interior circulation hub |
| Bathroom (1F) | "Bathroom / Bath / Bathroom 1F" | Extended to connect 1F Tellers walls (Crimson Heist rework) |
| Main Entrance interior | "Entrance / Main Hall" | Main ground entry interior circulation |
| 1F Hallway | "Hallway / 1F Hall" | Main passage connecting areas |
| Waiting Room | "Waiting" | Room circulation area |

### Exterior Locations
| Location | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| East Vehicle Entrance (spawn) | "East" | Northeast attacker spawn; exterior entry route |
| Valley (spawn) | "Valley / Mid" | Middle attacker spawn area |
| West Vehicle Exit (spawn) | "West" | Northwest attacker spawn; exterior exit area |
| Main Entrance (exterior) | "Front / Front Gate" | South-facing main exterior entry |
| East exterior side | "East Side / East Ext" | Right-side building exterior |
| West exterior side | "West Side / West Ext" | Left-side building exterior |
| General exterior | "Outside / Ext" | Exterior approach areas |

### Defender Spawn
| Location | Notes |
|---|---|
| Interior at map start | Defenders spawn inside checkpoint facility |

---

## Key Walls & Hatches

### Critical Reinforced Walls (prioritize reinforcing)
- **Armory Lockers entry walls (2F):** Control site access; reinforce to segment bomb areas
- **Archives to Offices walls (2F):** Divide defensive anchor positions across extended site
- **Security Room walls (2F):** Primary bomb area protection; reinforce to create defensive hold points
- **East Entrance access (2F):** Controls entry from stairs; key defensive chokepoint
- **Customs Inspection entry (1F):** Funnel attackers through controlled breach points
- **Workshop to Customs wall (1F):** Critical wall segmenting west/east 1F bomb sites
- **Ventilation room walls (1F):** Contained space; reinforce to prevent soft breaches
- **Tellers counter walls (1F):** Bathrooms extended (Crimson Heist), creates connection; reinforce for defense

### Key Destructible/Soft Walls (critical for vertical play)
- **Yellow Stairs floor/ceiling (2F to 1F):** Critical destructible vertical passage; soft destruction enables multi-floor pressure
- **Red Stairs floor/ceiling (2F to 1F):** Alternative vertical destruction route; soft walls in stairwell
- **2F floors over 1F (multiple locations):** Destructible ceilings enable vertical attacks from below
- **East Entrance external walls (2F):** Post-rework, exterior walls now covered internally; limited soft breach opportunity
- **Main Entrance soft walls (1F exterior):** Multiple soft walls provide exterior entry angles
- **Passport Check to main area walls (1F):** Soft walls reduced from 4 to 2 in Crimson Heist rework
- **Armory to Workshop wall (1F):** Soft wall connection between sites
- **Bathroom to Tellers wall (1F):** Extended bathroom creates new soft connection (Crimson Heist rework)

### Key Hatches (5 total per wiki)
- **2F Security Room to 1F Customs Inspection (ceiling hatch):** Vertical pressure from Security Room above into Customs
- **2F Security Room to 1F Customs Desk (ceiling hatch):** Second hatch from Security Room to ground-level Customs area
- **Yellow Stairs vertical passage:** Destructible floor/ceiling hatch connection between 2F and 1F
- **Red Stairs vertical passage:** East-side destructible vertical passage between floors
- **Additional vertical connections:** Between upper and lower levels for roamer access and pressure

---

## Camera Locations (Default)

- **Main Entrance exterior camera:** Covers south-side exterior approach and main entry spawn areas
- **Armory Lockers area camera (2F):** Positioned to monitor north 2F site approach and Archives access
- **Security Room area camera (2F):** Covers east 2F site and Security Room approach from stairs
- **East Stairs/East Entrance area camera (2F):** Monitors vertical stair approach to upper bomb site
- **Customs Inspection/Customs Desk area camera (1F):** Covers primary ground-level entry and site approach
- **Tellers/Customs area camera (1F):** Positioned to monitor east 1F bomb site
- **Workshop/Ventilation area camera (1F):** Covers west 1F industrial site approach
- **Exterior catwalks camera:** Positioned to monitor rooftop approaches and external pressure routes

---

## Attack Strategies & Key Angles

- **Multi-directional spawn pressure:** Three spawn points (East, Valley, West) enable simultaneous pressure from multiple angles, stretching roamer rotations. Coordinate spawn advantage to establish exterior control and force early defensive commitment.
- **Stairwell control priority:** Yellow Stairs and Red Stairs are primary vertical control points. Securing stairs early grants access to upper bomb sites and creates vertical pressure vectors. Red Stairs (east) enables direct East Entrance access; Yellow Stairs enables central map presence.
- **2F Security Room vertical pressure:** Two hatches from Security Room to 1F Customs areas (Customs Inspection and Customs Desk) enable coordinated multi-angle vertical pressure. Sequence hatch destruction to overwhelm defenders from above.
- **Soft wall exploitation (reduced post-rework):** Passport Check area soft walls reduced from 4 to 2 in Crimson Heist; identify remaining destruction opportunities to create flanking angles. East exterior walls now largely interior (post-rework); adapt breach strategy accordingly.
- **Exterior/rooftop pressure:** Catwalks and exterior approaches create constant external pressure. Coordinated repel from catwalks combined with ground-level push on multiple bomb sites forces defender spread.
- **Site-specific executes:** Customs Inspection/Tellers site (1F) often most accessible from Main Entrance; Armory Lockers/Archives (2F) requires stair control first. Execute highest-confidence site after establishing vertical/exterior dominance.
- **Utility denial before push:** Grenades and utility clear defender positions on stairs and site approaches before final execute. Coordinate utility timing with stair control to prevent defender reinforcement.

---

## Defense Strategies & Anchor Spots

- **2F Armory Lockers anchor (north site):** Defender holds Armory with locker cover; Archives extended design provides secondary anchor positions. Post-rework floor solid on external walls reduces vertical vulnerability.
- **2F Security Room anchor (east site):** Defender holds Security Room with furniture cover. Monitor two hatches (Customs Inspection and Customs Desk) for vertical pressure. Vertical defenders must manage simultaneous above-pressure vectors.
- **Stairwell deniers (Red/Yellow Stairs):** Roamers positioned at stair landing zones to intercept early vertical attacks. Red Stairs (east) and Yellow Stairs (central) are primary roamer intercept points. Utility (impacts, shields) creates difficult push angles.
- **1F Customs/Tellers anchor:** Anchor holds Customs Inspection or Tellers counter position with furniture cover. Bathroom extension (post-rework) creates new anchor option. Supply Room repositioned (post-rework) requires awareness of new rotation routes.
- **1F Workshop anchor (west site):** Anchor holds Workshop depth position among machinery cover. Ventilation as trap or secondary position. Limited traditional anchor options due to industrial space layout.
- **Multi-site rotation management:** Border features four distinct bomb objectives on two floors. Establish primary site anchor; maintain rotation flexibility for secondary site commitment. Use Armory and stair positions as rotation hubs.
- **Exterior defense:** Position defenders at 2F windows to deny exterior/catwalks pressure. Create overlapping coverage on external approaches; manage rappel points. East Entrance extended exterior wall coverage (post-rework) reduces external breach options.
- **Utility placement:** Shields and gadgets positioned at stair landings, site entries, and hallway chokepoints. Delay attackers before site reach; utility enables defender reinforcement rotation.

---

## Stack Performance Notes (Y10S4)

Border is the stack's dominant map. Sarge leads with 76.9% win rate, establishing Border as his map. All players maintain above 57% win rate: Hound 60.7%, Smigs 58.1%, Grant 57.6%, Slug 57.5% (B Team as of 2026-03-21; Peej data pending). The team has developed 2 full strategies with deep site understanding and strong roamer coordination. The stack's success on Border correlates with strong exterior pressure execution and effective stairwell control. No defensive weaknesses identified; team has strong fundamentals on this map. Consider Border a high-confidence map for draft/selection.

---

*Confidence: ✅ High — Verified via Rainbow Six Wiki, Mar 2026. Border room names, bomb site configurations (Armory/Archives, Security Room/Offices, Workshop/Ventilation, Customs/Tellers), and spawn locations (East, Valley, West) verified. Crimson Heist rework changes documented: 1F Bathroom extension, Supply Room relocation, Passport Check soft wall reduction (4→2), 2F Armory layout redesign, Archives extension, 2F hatches to 1F Customs areas (2 total hatch points) confirmed.*
