| Zipline area | Zipline / Zip | Exterior zipline connector ⚠️ Verify if actual map feature |
| Rappel points | Rappel / Drop | Window rappel access points |

---

## Key Walls & Hatches

### Critical Reinforced Walls
- **Server / Garage connecting wall (2F):** Typically reinforced to compartmentalize sites and control rotation between objectives
- **Electrical / Control connecting wall (2F):** Variable reinforcement; often left soft for rotation flexibility depending on site selection
- **Kitchen / Vending connecting wall (1F):** Usually reinforced to maintain distinct objective pressures
- **Cargo / Exo connecting wall (B):** Reinforced to create defensive compartments in basement isolation

### Key Soft Walls
- **Hallway walls (1F/2F):** Multiple soft wall options for rotation enhancement and roamer flexibility
- **Corridor walls (all floors):** Enable cross-corridor breaches and attacker confusion
- **Basement corridor walls:** Soft wall options for multi-vector pressure during basement attacks

### Key Hatches
- **2F to 1F hatches (Server/Garage area):** Multiple vertical access points; critical for upper site pressure
- **2F to 1F hatches (Electrical/Control area):** Secondary vertical axis
- **1F to Basement hatches (main circulation):** Direct basement access; enable combined vertical-basement pressure
- **Central facility hatch (main atrium):** Often a major vertical chokepoint

---

## Camera Locations (Default)

- **2F corridor camera:** Positioned to monitor hallway approach to upper sites
- **1F main camera:** Central placement covering Kitchen/Vending entry areas and main corridor
- **Basement camera:** Lower level monitoring near hatch or cargo area
- **Exterior camera:** Positioned to watch glass panel approaches, external rappel points, or main entrance
- **Valkyrie positioning:** Often placed on high-tech surfaces (glass panels, ceiling mounts, wall corners) for unusual angles. Placed to cover roamer rotation paths and exterior approaches. Difficult-to-destroy placement in hallway junctions is valuable.
- **Mozzie:** Typically deployed in main hallways (1F circulation, basement main corridor), near site entries, or on exterior to watch attacker approach routes and window breach points

---

## Attack Strategies & Key Angles

**Vertical Pressure (Primary Advantage):**
- Multiple hatches enable simultaneous vertical pressure — designate dedicated vertical player
- Open hatches early before full site execute to disrupt defender setup
- Vertical roamer pressure creates decision paralysis for basement-site defends

**Exterior Window Play:**
- Large exterior glass panels create multiple window breach opportunities — establish exterior control early
- Coordinate window breaches with interior entry to prevent defender reinforcement
- Exterior player placement critical; windows are monitored

**Site Execution:**
- After vertical pressure established, commit coordinated site breach with clear role definition
- Two-room sites require simultaneous or near-simultaneous room coverage
- Basement sites require heavy vertical support — secure all hatch areas before committing basin

**Key angles to watch:**
- Hallway junctions (roamer peek points at 1F/2F intersections)
- Hatch landing areas (vertical vulnerability)
- Window corners (exterior attacker position repeeking)
- Soft wall breach points (defender counter-breach)
- Stairwell transitions (defender holding vertical control points)

**Drone usage:**
- Scout each floor separately; establish whether vertical hatches are open/closed
- Reserve mid-round drones for real-time execute intel

---

## Defense Strategies & Anchor Spots

**Anchor Positioning (per site):**
- **Server Room/Garage (2F):** Anchor holds primary objective, manages window pressure actively with roamer support. Cannot both hold tight in room and manage exterior threat — roamer coordination critical.
- **Electrical/Control (2F):** Similar to Server/Garage; anchor holds site with exterior threat awareness. Soft wall rotations enable flexibility.
- **Kitchen/Vending (1F):** Anchor holds site with rear area access for rotation denial. More stable than upper sites due to less vertical vulnerability. Roamer can play adjacent corridor.
- **Cargo Bay/Exo Storage (B):** Anchor consolidates in basement; highly vulnerable to vertical pressure. Roamer above (near hatch) critical for vertical threat awareness and denial.

**Roamer Routes:**
- **Upper roamer (2F):** Plays adjacent site areas; controls hallway junctions and manages exterior window pressure denial
- **Mid roamer (1F):** Plays main circulation areas; controls corridor junctions and stairwell
- **Vertical roamer:** Advanced roamer focused on hatch control and denying vertical attacks; positions above anticipated attack sites
- **Exterior roamer:** If applicable, plays exterior to contest window breaches and manage glass panel areas

**Vertical Defense:**
- Never leave hatches fully undefended — assign one player hatch monitoring responsibility
- Basement sites are highly vulnerable; anticipate multiple simultaneous hatches during execute
- Soft wall placement above sites can reduce vertical exposure
- Coordination between anchor and roamer critical for vertical pressure response

---

## Stack Performance Notes (Y10S4)

| Player | Operator | Win Rate | K/D | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Smigs | — | 100% | — | Dominant performance, small sample (1-2 games) |
| Sarge | — | Strong | — | Positive results, effective vertical usage |
| Grant | — | 28.6% | — | Weak performance, struggles with vertical/exterior commitment |
| Hound | — | 33.3% | — | Weak performance, similar difficulty pattern |
| Slug | — | Low % | — | Inconsistent results ⚠️ Exact data unclear *(B Team as of 2026-03-21; historical data)* |
| Peej | — | _TBD_ | — | New to main stack 2026-03-21; no prior Nighthaven Labs data |

**Notes:** Nighthaven Labs is extremely polarizing. Smigs and Sarge excel (vertical positioning, exterior play), while Grant and Hound struggle significantly. This suggests the map rewards specific play patterns or positioning skills. Two strategies developed but high variance in execution. Recommend analysis of Smigs/Sarge vertical setup vs. Grant/Hound approach to identify playable strategies for entire team.

---

*Confidence: ✅ Medium-High — Verified via Rainbow Six Wiki (Operation Solar Raid, Y7S4 2022). Layout confirmed: Basement, 1F, 2F, Rooftop. Core callout names verified (Server, Garage, Electrical, Control, Kitchen, Vending, Cargo, Exo are standard competitive callouts). Map location Singapore verified. Wiki indicates "many entry points and breach points" supporting existing KB's emphasis on exterior/window play. Recommend verification of: basement layout complexity, exact exterior zone callouts, and secondary room naming conventions. Flag with ⚠️: Zipline feature existence, Research/Lab basement area separation, multi-corridor routing details.*

strats.content:
# Oregon — Main Dorms Hall / Kids Dormitory (2F) · ATTACK

**Status:** ⚠️ Partially Developed (migrated from STACK_03 2026-03-14)
**Formation:** TBD
**Developed:** 2026-03-14 (migrated from STACK_03 inline content)

> **Site Context:** Main Dorms/Kids Dorm is the 2F bomb site. Multiple windows create significant external pressure. Attic connection provides rooftop/exterior approach vulnerability. Iconic spawn peek vulnerability documented. This site is frequently attacked; defenders hold it strongly.

---

## Spawn Peek Risk Warning

⚠️ **CRITICAL:** Attic windows are a primary spawn peek threat. Grant died from spawn peek during strat development. **Approach this site assuming Attic hold is defended.** Early utility (smokes, flashes) essential for safety on approach. Do not rely on speed; use utility to suppress Attic/window positions before advancing.

---

## Spawn

_TBD_ — Spawn selection not specified in source. Select spawn enabling safe approach to site given spawn peek risk.

---

## Roles

| Player | Role | Operator | Assignment |
|--------|------|----------|-----------|
| **All 5** | Rush | _TBD_ | Full team coordinated push into site. Note: source states "All 5: Rush" — interpret as aggressive coordinated entry, not individual aggression. |

**Source Note:** Strat development indicates simple "rush" execute — all 5 players coordinate simultaneous aggressive entry into site. Exact operator assignments and role differentiation _TBD_.

**Operator Assignments:** ⚠️ **ALL assignments marked TBD** — Source provides only "rush" designation. Assign operators based on:
- Spawn peek counter-utility (smokes, flashes, or stun options)
- Hard breach capability (Thermite/Ace)
- Anti-gadget (Thatcher)
- Site clearing utility

---

## Execute Sequence

> ⚠️ **Incomplete source data.** Below sequence is logically inferred from "rush" designation and spawn peek risk. Flesh out with actual team callouts and timing.

1. **Approach phase** — All 5 players advance together from spawn. **Early utility deployment:** All players throw smokes/flashes at Attic windows and general exterior areas to suppress spawn peek positions. Do not sprint into exposed windows.

2. **Window control** — Establish window control via utility suppression. Identify and suppress Attic/Kids Dorm window holds before main site entry.

3. **Main Dorms/Kids Dorm entry** — All 5 push simultaneously into site. Breach both Main Dorms and Kids Dorm entries (or primary entry, depending on defender setup).

4. **Utility denial** — Hard breach on site walls (if reinforced). Clear hatches/vertical threats. Deny defender gadgets on entry points.

5. **Site takeover** — Full team secures site. Establish crossfire control and deny roamer counter-push.

6. **Plant** — Plant in preferred location with cover. Likely Main Dorms or Kids Dorm depending on defender setup and site control.

---

## Plant Position (PP)

**Primary:** _TBD_ — Likely Main Dorms or Kids Dorm (source does not specify preferred plant spot)
**Alternate:** _TBD_

⚠️ **Mark specific plant spots in-game based on defender setup and window exposure.**

---

## Post-Plant

| Player | Position | Job |
|--------|----------|-----|
| _TBD_ | _TBD_ | _TBD_ |
| _TBD_ | _TBD_ | _TBD_ |
| _TBD_ | _TBD_ | _TBD_ |
| _TBD_ | _TBD_ | _TBD_ |
| _TBD_ | _TBD_ | _TBD_ |

---

## Watch For

- **Attic spawn peek** — Primary documented threat. Window positions must be suppressed before main entry. If spawn peek occurs, respond with utility and immediate site rush to overwhelm defenders.

- **Attic roamer pressure** — Defenders may hold Attic window position aggressively to catch pushers. Early utility suppression (smokes, flashes) followed by immediate site push minimizes roamer impact.

- **Hallway roamer** — Secondary roamer may hold Hallway junction. Account for this during main entry.

- **Kids Dorm roamer flank** — Defenders may position roamer in Kids Dorm to flank Main Dorms push. Clear Kids Dorm simultaneously with Main Dorms entry to prevent flank.

- **Bathroom roamer** — Alternative roamer position. Identify during approach.

---

## Win Conditions

### Win A — Primary: Spawn Peek Suppressed, Full Site Rush ✅
**When:** All 5 throw early utility at Attic/windows, defenders cannot spawn peek, site entry succeeds.

Execute full team simultaneous push. Suppress Attic positions with utility. Rush site. Plant. Win.

**Who calls it:** IGL confirms Attic suppressed and clears team to execute.

---

### Win B — Spawn Peek Occurs, Adapt to Gunfight ⚠️
**When:** Early spawn peek kills a player despite utility, defenders get picks on approach.

**Adjustment:**
- Do not abandon execute. Adapt to reduced numbers (5v4 or worse).
- Fall back to perimeter hold if heavily disadvantaged.
- Wait for utility refresh if available.
- Re-position around cover and re-engage from different angle.
- Prioritize not dying to spawn peeks — minimize wasted rounds.

**Who calls it:** IGL identifies lost players and calls adjusted approach.

---

### Win C — Site Denied, Fall Back to Perimeter ⚠️
**When:** Hard defenders lock site, roamers push early, team cannot breach main doors/walls.

**Adjustment:**
- Fall back to perimeter hold outside site.
- Establish spawn control.
- Wait for utility refresh (grenades, smokes).
- Re-engage from stronger position.
- Do not waste utility trying to force a denied entry.

---

## Audibles

- **Attic spawn peek confirmed:** Respond with targeted utility. IGL calls "Attic suppressed" before team rushes.

- **Unexpected roamer early:** Fall back to perimeter. Re-position and re-engage.

- **Multiple defenders on windows:** Adjust utility allocation — focus smokes on deadliest window positions first.

---

## Development Notes & TODOs

⚠️ **This strategy is incomplete and requires team refinement:**

- [ ] Define specific operator selections for each role (currently TBD)
- [ ] Flesh out Post-Plant positioning and holdout angles
- [ ] Define concrete spawn peek mitigation timing and utility allocation
- [ ] Specify plant locations (Main Dorms vs. Kids Dorm preference)
- [ ] Define specific Win Conditions and audibles based on actual team practice
- [ ] Record actual execute sequence timing from team practice
- [ ] Test spawn peek suppression effectiveness with actual utility combinations
- [ ] Document roamer threat areas and how team will clear them during rush

**Next Steps:** Migrate this strategy to team practice with emphasis on spawn peek suppression. Record actual timings, utility effectiveness, and roamer positioning. Refine plant spots and post-plant holds based on live play.

---

_Last migrated: 2026-03-14 from STACK_03 inline strat. Marked as partially developed pending team refinement. Note: Grant died from spawn peek during strat dev — suggests strat needs spawn peek suppression refinement._

overviewContent:
# Oregon — Overview

⚠️ **Y11S1 REWORK NOTICE:** Oregon was modernized in Operation Silent Hunt (Y11S1). Room names and layout below are based on verified callouts and floor plan images. Verify in-game before relying in match.

**Environment:** Classic militia compound; rustic house with barn structure and underground utility areas
**Floors:** Basement / Underground (B), Ground Floor (1F), Upper Floor / Attic (2F)
**In Y11S1 Ranked Pool:** ✅ Yes — first half of season only. Exits at Y11S1 mid-season swap (replaced by undisclosed maps).
**Year Added / Reworked:** Original map (Y1S0); major rework Operation Void Edge (Y5S2, March 2020); Y11S1 modernization rework

---

## Overview

Oregon is a classic competitive map featuring a main residential building with attached barn/utility structure and basement areas. The tight, close-quarters layout favors defenders with well-coordinated positioning and roaming. Multiple soft walls enable flexible rotations and site setup variations. The map's iconic design features distinct elevation changes, tight hallways, and limited long sightlines. Attackers require methodical clear operations and coordinated site execution.

Oregon has been a strong performer for the stack historically, with positive win rates across most players. However, **Grant's recent priorities flag Oregon as a WEAK map this season (6M/33.3% WR).** Despite the Y10S4 strong historical record, the current team performance has deteriorated. **Do NOT auto-protect Oregon in bans.** Reassess ban strategy based on current meta and team comfort.

One partial strategy developed (likely ATK_Attic_Bedroom from STACK_03 inline content, noting spawn peek vulnerability and Grant death during strat dev). Three bomb sites remain underdeveloped. Recommend documenting strategies with emphasis on Attic spawn peek mitigations.

---

## Bomb Sites

### Site 1 — Main Dorms Hall / Kids Dormitory — 2F
**Rooms:** Main Dorms Hall (1A — main dormitory) / Kids Dormitory (1B — children's bedroom)
**Description:** Upper floor site with iconic spawn peek vulnerability documented in strat dev. Main Dorms Hall is spacious with multiple beds and furniture providing cover; Kids Dormitory is smaller bedroom area. Multiple windows create significant external pressure. Attic connection provides rooftop/exterior approach vulnerability. Site is frequently attacked and heavily defended against.
**Common defense:** One anchor holds interior Main Dorms, with dedicated roamer holding Kids Dorm/Attic area for potential spawn peeks. Multiple soft wall rotations enable aggressive defender positioning. Window defense critical.
**Attack approach:** Account for spawn peek threat early in approach. Clear Kids Dorm/Attic carefully; prioritize window control before interior breach. Main Dorms Hall breach requires soft wall clearing and utility denial. Use utility to suppress defenders from window positions.
**⚠️ Spawn Peek Risk:** Documented vulnerability in existing partial strat. Grant died from spawn peek during strat development — flag this site as having spawn peek risk. Early utility (smokes, flashes) essential for approach safety.

### Site 2 — Dining Hall / Kitchen — 1F
**Rooms:** Dining Hall (2A — dining area) / Kitchen (2B — shared utility area)
**Description:** Ground floor site featuring dining and food preparation areas. Dining Hall is moderately open with dining furniture; Kitchen offers utility storage and tight angles. Central location with good rotation access through soft walls. Moderate attacker commitment required. Shared Kitchen area with alternate Meeting Hall site (Site 3).
**Common defense:** Anchor typically holds Kitchen for tight angles; one roamer plays adjacent Laundry/Bathroom for rotation denial. Soft wall reinforcement variable depending on site defense strategy and attacker location prediction.
**Attack approach:** Clear adjacent Laundry/Bathroom first to deny roamer flanks. Breach both Dining and Kitchen simultaneously or in quick succession. Multiple entry points from hallways and exterior access. Exterior breaches possible via barn/Watch Tower area.

### Site 3 — Meeting Hall / Kitchen — 1F
**Rooms:** Meeting Hall (3A — meeting/operations room) / Kitchen (3B — shared utility area)
**Description:** Alternate 1F bomb site pairing using shared Kitchen area with alternate primary room. Meeting Hall is a tactical operations/briefing room with different defensive angles than Dining Hall. Creates significant setup flexibility — both 1F sites share Kitchen but have different primary rooms. Attacker difficulty: early drone identification is critical to determine which 1F site is hit.
**Common defense:** Can anchor in Kitchen for either 1F site; Meeting Hall offers distinct defensive angles compared to Dining Hall. Roamer coverage varies based on which site is hit. Kitchen remains central defensive position.
**Attack approach:** Early drone identification critical to determine which 1F site is hit. Approach varies significantly — Dining Hall requires Laundry approach while Meeting Hall has different corridor vectors. Kitchen clearing must be flexible for both site possibilities.

### Site 4 — Laundry Room / Supply Room — Basement
**Rooms:** Laundry Room (4A — laundry/utility) / Supply Room (4B — supply storage)
**Description:** Basement level site with tight corridors and limited visibility. Laundry Room offers utility focus; Supply Room is storage-focused. Underground positioning makes defender rotations to upper floors time-consuming and resource-intensive. Multiple hatch access from above creates strong vertical vulnerability.
**Common defense:** Anchor holds basement area, typically consolidates between Laundry/Supply rooms. Roamer plays upper floor basement stairs/entry points for vertical pressure denial. Communication critical due to vertical isolation.
**Attack approach:** Vertical pressure from above is major advantage — hatches should be primary entry vector. Target basement stairs entry early to secure access routes. Coordinate simultaneous hatches with ground level basement stairs approach for split defense. Underground nature enables rapid site takeover if vertical control established.

---

## Room Names & Community Callouts

### 2F (Upper Floor / Attic) — Verified
| Room | Callout | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| Main Dorms Hall | Main Dorms / Dorms | Bomb site (1A); upper floor dormitory; spawn peek vulnerability |
| Kids Dormitory | Kids / Kids Dorm | Bomb site (1B); children's bedroom; adjacent to Main Dorms |
| Attic | Attic | Upper floor storage; exposed windows; CRITICAL spawn peek position |
| Hallway | Hall / Hallway / 2F Hall | Main 2F circulation corridor |
| Bathroom | Bathroom | Utility room; central to multiple areas |
| Stairs | Stairs / Main Stairs | Primary vertical access to 1F |

### 1F (Ground Floor) — Verified
| Room | Callout | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| Dining Hall | Dining / Dining Room | Bomb site (2A); primary dining area |
| Meeting Hall | Meeting / Meeting Room | Bomb site (3A); alternate 1F site; meeting/operations room |
| Kitchen | Kitchen / Kit | Bomb site (2B, 3B); shared between both 1F sites; central utility |
| Laundry | Laundry / Wash | Common roamer position; 1F utility area |
| Bathroom | Bathroom | Utility restroom; central to Kitchen/Dining area |
| Living Area | Living / Main | Central living space; circulation hub |
| Hallway | Hall / Main Hall | Central circulation corridor |
| Basement Stairs | Basement Stairs / Down | Vertical descent to Basement; tight chokepoint |
| Exterior (barn area) | Barn / Outside | Attached barn structure; exterior pressure |
| Watch Tower | Watch Tower / Tower | Exterior tower structure; rappel access |

### Basement / Underground (B) — Floor Plan Labeled
| Room | Callout | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| Laundry Room | Laundry / Wash | Bomb site (4A); basement laundry/utility |
| Supply Room | Supply | Bomb site (4B); supply storage; hatch from Kitchen above |
| Basement Hall / Basement Corridor | Basement / Base Corridor | Main basement circulation |
| Utility Area | Utility / Utilities / Electric | Support systems area |
| Storage | Storage | Equipment storage basement area |
| Back Stairs | Back Stairs | Rear stairwell; basement to exterior |

⚠️ **Floor Plan Basement Labels:** Basement image shows many utility area names (Pillar, Boiler, Electric, Bunker, Barrels, Closet, Back Laundry, Laundry Storage, Freezer, Freezer Stairs). Reconcile these floor plan labels against verified in-game callouts — Y11S1 rework may have changed naming.

### Exterior / Attacker Spawns
| Location | Callout | Notes |
|----------|---------|-------|
| Main front | Front / Outside | Primary exterior entrance |
| Back area | Back / Rear | Rear of main building |
| Barn exterior | Barn | Attached barn/utility structure |
| Watch Tower | Watch Tower / Tower | Tall external structure with rappel points |
| Side areas | Side / Left / Right | Building sides |

---

## Key Walls & Hatches

### Critical Reinforced Walls
- **Attic / Bedroom wall (2F):** Typically reinforced to separate objectives; controls sight lines between rooms
- **Kitchen / Dining connecting wall (1F):** Variable reinforcement — often left soft for roamer rotation flexibility
- **Kitchen / Meeting connecting wall (1F):** Similar reinforcement strategy; depends on which 1F site is hit
- **Basement stair entry wall (1F → B):** Reinforced to deny direct vertical line of sight and slow basement entry
- **Laundry / Supply division wall (Basement):** Reinforced to create defensive compartments

### Key Soft Walls
- **Hallway walls (all floors):** Multiple soft wall options enabling roamer rotation and attacker breach points
- **Laundry walls (1F):** Soft walls create roamer movement flexibility and attacker site isolation
- **Bathroom walls (1F/2F):** Multiple soft options for rotation enhancement
- **Basement corridor walls (B):** Soft walls enable multi-vector basement approaches

### Key Hatches
- **2F to 1F hatch (main stairwell):** Primary vertical connection; contested hatch position
- **1F to Basement hatch (Kitchen area):** Direct basement access; critical for vertical pressure coordination
- **Additional basement hatches:** Floor plan shows multiple connections; verify exact locations in-game

referenceContent:
# Oregon — Map Reference

**Environment:** Classic militia compound; rustic house with barn structure and underground utility areas
**Floors:** Basement / Underground (B), Ground Floor (1F), Upper Floor / Attic (2F)
**In Y10S4 Ranked Pool:** Yes
**Year Added / Reworked:** Original map (Y1S0); major rework Operation Void Edge (Y5S2, March 2020); minor adjustments Y5+

---

## Overview

Oregon is a classic competitive map featuring a main residential building with attached barn/utility structure and basement areas. The tight, close-quarters layout favors defenders with well-coordinated positioning and roaming. Multiple soft walls enable flexible rotations and site setup variations. The map's iconic design features distinct elevation changes, tight hallways, and limited long sightlines. Attackers require methodical clear operations and coordinated site execution. Generally defender-favored; the stack shows strong results, with significant roaming opportunity and spawn peek vulnerabilities on the Attic/Bedroom site documented in strategies.

---

## Bomb Sites

### Site: Main Dorms Hall / Kids Dormitory — 2F
**Rooms:** Main Dorms Hall (primary bomb site A, main dormitory space) / Kids Dormitory (bomb site B, children's bedroom area)
**Description:** Upper floor site with iconic spawn peek vulnerability. Main Dorms Hall is spacious with multiple beds and furniture providing cover; Kids Dormitory is smaller bedroom area. Multiple windows create significant external pressure. Attic connection provides rooftop/exterior approach vulnerability. This site is frequently attacked and heavily defended against.
**Common defense:** Typically one anchor holds interior Main Dorms, with dedicated roamer holding Kids Dorm/Attic area for potential spawn peeks. Multiple soft wall rotations enable aggressive defender positioning. Window defense critical.
**Attack approach:** Account for spawn peek threat early in approach. Clear Kids Dorm/Attic carefully; prioritize window control before interior breach. Main Dorms Hall breach requires soft wall clearing and utility denial. Use utility to suppress defenders from window positions.

### Site: Dining Hall / Kitchen — 1F
**Rooms:** Dining Hall (primary dining area, bomb site A) / Kitchen (food preparation area, bomb site B)
**Description:** Ground floor site featuring dining and food preparation areas. Dining Hall is moderately open with dining furniture; Kitchen offers utility storage and tight angles. Central location with good rotation access through soft walls. Moderate attacker commitment required.
**Common defense:** Anchor typically holds Kitchen for tight angles, one roamer plays adjacent Laundry/Bathroom for rotation denial. Soft wall reinforcement variable depending on site defense strategy and attacker location prediction.
**Attack approach:** Clear adjacent Laundry/Bathroom first to deny roamer flanks. Breach both Dining and Kitchen simultaneously or in quick succession. Multiple entry points from hallways and exterior access. Exterior breaches possible via barn/Watch Tower area.

### Site: Meeting Hall / Kitchen — 1F
**Rooms:** Meeting Hall (meeting/operations room, bomb site A) / Kitchen (food preparation area, bomb site B, shared)
**Description:** Alternate 1F bomb site pairing using shared Kitchen area with alternate primary room. Meeting Hall is a tactical operations/briefing room with different defensive angles than Dining Hall. Creates significant setup flexibility — both 1F sites share Kitchen but have different primary rooms.
**Common defense:** Can anchor in Kitchen for either 1F site; Meeting Hall offers distinct defensive angles compared to Dining Hall. Roamer coverage varies based on which site is hit. Kitchen remains central defensive position.
**Attack approach:** Early drone identification is critical to determine which 1F site is hit. Approach varies significantly — Dining Hall requires Laundry approach while Meeting Hall has different corridor vectors. Kitchen clearing must be flexible for both site possibilities.

### Site: Laundry Room / Supply Room — Basement
**Rooms:** Laundry Room (laundry/utility area, bomb site A) / Supply Room (supply storage, bomb site B)
**Description:** Basement level site with tight corridors and limited visibility. Laundry Room offers utility focus; Supply Room is storage-focused. Underground positioning makes defender rotations to upper floors time-consuming and resource-intensive. Multiple hatch access from above creates strong vertical vulnerability.
**Common defense:** Anchor holds basement area, typically consolidates between Laundry/Supply rooms. Roamer plays upper floor basement stairs/entry points for vertical pressure denial. Communication critical due to vertical isolation. Vertical roamer essential for hatch defense.
**Attack approach:** Vertical pressure from above is major advantage — hatches should be primary entry vector. Target basement stairs entry early to secure access routes. Coordinate simultaneous hatches with ground level basement stairs approach for split defense. Underground nature enables rapid site takeover if vertical control established.

---

## Room Names & Community Callouts

### 2F (Upper Floor / Attic)
| Room / Location | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Main Dorms Hall | Main Dorms / Dorms | Upper floor main dormitory space, bomb site A |
| Kids Dormitory | Kids / Kids Dorm | Children's bedroom area, bomb site B |
| Attic | Attic | Upper floor storage, exposed windows, spawn peek location |
| Hallway | Hall / Hallway | Main 2F circulation corridor |
| Bathroom | Bathroom | Utility room |
| Stairs | Stairs / Main Stairs | Primary vertical access |

### 1F (Ground Floor)
| Room / Location | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Dining Hall | Dining / Dining Room | Primary dining area, bomb site A (Dining/Kitchen pair) |
| Meeting Hall | Meeting / Meeting Room | Meeting/operations room, bomb site A (Meeting/Kitchen pair) |
| Kitchen | Kitchen / Kit | Food preparation area, shared between both 1F bomb sites |
| Laundry | Laundry / Wash | Laundry room, common roamer position |
| Bathroom | Bathroom | Utility room |
| Living area | Living / Main | Central living space, circulation |
| Hallway | Hall / Main Hall | Central circulation corridor |
| Exterior (barn area) | Barn / Outside | Attached barn structure exterior |
| Watch Tower entrance | Watch Tower / Tower | Entrance to exterior watchtower rappel |

### Basement / Underground (B)
| Room / Location | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Laundry Room | Laundry / Wash | Laundry/utility basement area, bomb site A |
| Supply Room | Supply | Supply storage room, bomb site B |
| Basement Corridor | Basement / Base Corridor | Main basement circulation |
| Utility area | Utility / Utilities | Support systems area |
| Storage | Storage | Equipment storage area |
| Stairs | Basement Stairs / Down | Vertical access to 1F |

### Exterior
| Location | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Main front | Front / Outside | Primary exterior entrance |
| Back area | Back / Rear | Rear of main building |
| Barn exterior | Barn | Attached barn/utility structure |
| Watch Tower | Watch Tower / Tower | Tall external structure with rappel points |
| Side areas | Side / Left / Right | Building sides |
| Rooftop | Roof / Rooftop | Main building roof (limited access) |

---

## Key Walls & Hatches

### Critical Reinforced Walls
- **Attic / Bedroom wall (2F):** Typically reinforced to separate objectives, controls sight lines between rooms
- **Kitchen / Dining connecting wall (1F):** Variable reinforcement — often left soft for roamer rotation flexibility
- **Kitchen / Meeting connecting wall (1F):** Similar reinforcement strategy; depends on which 1F site is hit
- **Construction / Freezer connecting wall (B):** Reinforced to create basement defensive compartments
- **Basement / 1F wall:** Reinforced to deny direct vertical line of sight from basement to ground floor

### Key Soft Walls
- **Hallway walls (all floors):** Multiple soft wall options enabling roamer rotation and attacker breach points
- **Laundry walls (1F):** Soft walls create roamer movement flexibility and attacker site isolation
- **Bathroom walls (1F/2F):** Multiple soft options for rotation enhancement
- **Basement corridor walls (B):** Soft walls enable multi-vector basement approaches

### Key Hatches
- **2F to 1F hatch (main stairwell):** Primary vertical connection; contested hatch position
- **1F to B hatch (Kitchen area):** Direct basement access; critical for vertical pressure coordination
- **Secondary hatches:** Additional hatch options depending on basement layout details
- **Attic window exit (not hatch):** Used for rooftop rotations, not traditional hatch

---

## Camera Locations (Default)

- **Attic exterior camera:** Positioned near Attic windows to watch exterior approaches and spawn peek routes
- **Kitchen area camera:** Central 1F placement covering Kitchen/Dining entry
- **Basement camera:** Lower level monitoring near hatch or Construction area
- **Living area camera (1F):** Secondary 1F coverage watching adjacent hallway
- **Valkyrie positioning:** Often placed in hallways for unusual angles, on second floor overhangs, or Attic window positions for rooftop coverage. Placed to support roamer rotations and watch approaches to objective areas.
- **Mozzie:** Typically deployed in hallways (main 1F hall, Laundry area), near site entries, basement hatch area, or exterior near Watch Tower for spawn detection

---

## Attack Strategies & Key Angles

**Spawn Peek Awareness (Attic/Bedroom site critical):**
- Establish spawn protection early before breaching toward objective
- Account for Attic windows as primary spawn peek threat
- Use utility (smokes, flashes) to deny Attic window holds before executing
- Establish window control before committing Bedroom breach

**Methodical Site Clear:**
- Adjacent room clears (Laundry for Kitchen/Dining; secondary rooms for Attic) must precede main site pressure
- Multiple soft wall breaches during clear create roamer confusion
- Vertical pressure from Attic/Bedroom hatch is supplementary but valuable for basement sites

**Site Execution:**
- Two-room sites require coordinated timing — prevent defender retreat between rooms
- Kitchen sites: Both 1F sites share Kitchen; early identification crucial for roamer/utility positioning
- Basement sites: Vertical pressure from above combined with ground entry creates split defense

**Key angles to watch:**
- Attic/Bedroom windows (spawn peek and roamer angles)
- Hallway junctions (roamer ambush points)
- Laundry corners (common roamer position)
- Stairwell landing areas (vertical control points)
- Soft wall breach points (defender counter-breach during clear)
- Bathroom corners (tight angle holding)

**Drone usage:**
- Scout Attic first to identify window holds and spawn peek threat
- Reserve drones for mid-round site execute intel

---

## Defense Strategies & Anchor Spots

**Anchor Positioning (per site):**
- **Attic/Bedroom (2F):** Anchor holds interior Bedroom for tight angle defense. Dedicated roamer must hold Attic window position for potential spawn peeks (documented vulnerability). Cannot hold both alone — role clarity essential.
- **Kitchen/Dining (1F):** Anchor holds Kitchen for tight angles and center-of-map position. Roamer plays Laundry/Bathroom for rotation denial. Good rotation access to support Basement if needed.
- **Kitchen/Meeting (1F):** Anchor holds Kitchen similarly. Roamer adjusts positioning based on Meeting Room vs. Dining Room threat identification. Same kitchen anchor position works for both sites.
- **Construction/Freezer (B):** Anchor consolidates in basement; must communicate vertical pressure from upper floors. Roamer plays near basement stairs for vertical threat awareness.

**Roamer Routes:**
- **Attic roamer (2F):** CRITICAL ROLE — holds Attic window position for spawn peeks and exterior denial. Feeds intel on Attic/Bedroom breach attempts.
- **Laundry roamer (1F):** Plays Laundry area and adjacent hallways, creates Kitchen/Dining site pressure from flank. Can rotate to basement stairs for vertical denial.
- **Kids Room roamer:** Alternative 2F roamer position for intel gathering on hallway pressure
- **Basement roamer:** Advanced roamer holding basement stairs area for vertical pressure denial
- **Vertical roamer:** Plays 1F basement stairs/hatch area to deny vertical pressure during basement site attacks

**Roaming Coordination:**
- Heavy roaming is viable on Oregon due to multiple rotation paths and Laundry/hallway holding positions
- Roamers should maintain communication for coordinated defender pressure
- Watch Tower proximity enables rear rotations

---

## Stack Performance Notes (Y10S4)

⚠️ **Roster change 2026-03-21:** Peej promoted to main stack; Slug moved to B Team. Table below reflects historical Y10S4 data with Slug as 5th main stack player. Peej Y10S4 Oregon data pending.

| Player | Operator | Win Rate | K/D | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Grant | — | 71.4% | 1.96 | Strong performance, solid fundamentals |
| Smigs | — | 71.4% | — | Excellent results, consistent performance |
| Slug | — | 60.9% | — | Solid results, effective play *(B Team as of 2026-03-21; historical data)* |
| Peej | — | _TBD_ | — | New to main stack 2026-03-21; no Y10S4 data |
| Sarge | — | — | — | ⚠️ Data unclear for Oregon specifically |

**Notes:** Oregon is a strong map for the stack overall. All players show positive performance. One partial strategy developed (likely Attic/Bedroom); three sites remain strategically undeveloped despite strong statistical performance. Opportunity to formalize strategies leveraging consistent win rates. Attic/Bedroom site has documented spawn peek vulnerability; existing partial strat likely addresses this. Recommend strategy development for Kitchen/Dining, Kitchen/Meeting, and Construction/Freezer sites.

---

*Confidence: ✅ High — Verified via Rainbow Six Wiki, Mar 2026. Bomb sites confirmed: Main Dorms Hall & Kids Dormitory (2F), Dining Hall & Kitchen (1F), Meeting Hall & Kitchen (1F), Laundry Room & Supply Room (Basement). Reworked Operation Void Edge (Y5S2, March 2020). Core callouts standardized (Dorms, Kids, Dining, Meeting, Kitchen, Laundry, Supply, Watch Tower verified). Classic map with community-standard callouts. Spawn peek vulnerability at Attic/2F confirmed.*

referenceContent:
# Outback — Map Reference

**Environment:** Australian roadside motel/service station complex; distinctive dual-structure building with roadhouse/restaurant and motel sections in remote outback setting
**Floors:** Ground Floor (1F), Upper Floor / Motel Rooms (2F), Roof (accessible)
**In Y10S4 Ranked Pool:** Yes
**Year Added / Reworked:** Added Y4S1 (2019); reworked Y7S3 (High Calibre); further balance adjustments in Y7+

---

## Overview

Outback is a distinctive two-section structure featuring a roadhouse/restaurant complex (west side) and motel rooms (east side) connected via interior hallways. The dual-structure layout creates interesting attack/defense dynamics with distinct building identities. The roadhouse features larger open spaces with multiple windows (notable for spawn peeks), while the motel side has compact rooms with limited rotation options. The map is highly destructible with multiple soft walls, mezzanines in both Garage and Restaurant areas, and emphasis on vertical gameplay. Three attacker spawn locations (Fuel Pumps, Storage Yard, Camping/Roof) provide flexible approach options. The Y7S3 rework (High Calibre expansion) restructured the Garage section and adjusted soft wall placements. Stack shows solid overall performance with opportunity to formalize successful patterns into strategic frameworks.

---

## Bomb Sites

### Site 1: Piano Room / Laundry — 2F (Motel Side)
**Rooms:** Piano Room (motel entertainment area) and Laundry (washing/utility area)
**Description:** Upper floor motel site with distinct character spaces. Piano Room offers event/entertainment aesthetic; Laundry is compact utility space. Moderate vertical vulnerability through hatches. Accessible primarily from interior motel hallway connections.
**Common defense:** Anchor holds one room depending on objective layout; roamer must monitor vertical hatch threats from below. Tight motel corridor limits rotation flexibility.
**Attack approach:** Vertical pressure through hatches is primary advantage. Ground-level interior stairwell access supplementary. Coordinated upper/lower pressure overwhelms defenders in confined motel layout.

### Site 2: Party Room / Office — 2F (Administrative/Entertainment Side)
**Rooms:** Party Room (gathering/event space) and Office (administrative/coordination area)
**Description:** Upper floor administrative and entertainment areas. Party Room is spacious event area; Office provides smaller administrative space. Connected via interior hallways. Multiple soft walls for rotation flexibility.
**Common defense:** Anchor positioning varies by objective layout. Adjacent corridor control important for defender support. Moderate vertical vulnerability through connected hatches.
**Attack approach:** Clear adjacent offices and hallway areas first to deny roamer flanks. Multi-angle pressure on both objective rooms. Coordinate with vertical pressure if hatch access available.

### Site 3: Green Bedroom / Red Bedroom — 1F (Motel Ground Side)
**Rooms:** Green Bedroom (motel room A) and Red Bedroom (motel room B)
**Description:** Ground floor motel rooms with residential aesthetic. Two distinct bedroom spaces, relatively compact. Accessible from motel-side interior hallway and exterior motel section. Bathroom connecting area. Adjacent rooms offer rotation flexibility.
**Common defense:** Anchor consolidates between two bedroom spaces. Roamer plays motel hallway for entry denial and flank coverage. Interior connection to main building provides rotation support.
**Attack approach:** Clear motel hallway and adjacent areas first. Two-room objective requires coordinated entry from both exterior and interior vectors. Exterior motel side pressure supplements interior clear.

### Site 4: Mechanic Shop / Kitchen — 1F (Roadhouse/Utility Side)
**Rooms:** Mechanic Shop (garage/work area, renamed from previous Bike Repair) and Kitchen (food preparation area)
**Description:** Ground floor utility and food service areas. Mechanic Shop is work/industrial space; Kitchen is food preparation area. Multiple exterior doors and window access create external pressure vulnerability. Good rotation through roadhouse area. Rework consolidated several garage-area rooms into cleaner Mechanic Shop layout.
**Common defense:** Anchor holds one room depending on setup. Roamer plays adjacent Shark/bar areas and hallways for rotation denial. Exterior pressure is primary vulnerability.
**Attack approach:** Clear adjacent Shark Bar and restaurant areas first to deny roamer pressure. Coordinate multi-angle breach on both objective rooms. Exterior driveway/window pressure supplements interior clear. Rework layout enables more tactical flexibility.

---

## Room Names & Community Callouts

### 1F (Ground Floor)

**Roadhouse/Restaurant Section (West)**
| Room / Location | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Shark Bar / Shark | Shark Bar / Shark | Secondary bar area, common roamer position |
| Restaurant / Main | Restaurant / Shark Area | Main restaurant/dining space |
| Kitchen | Kitchen / Kit | Food preparation area, objective room |
| Main Bar / Bull Bar Area | Bar / Bull Bar | Primary bar counter area (reference from pre-rework) |
| Bathroom | Bathroom | Utility room |
| Mezzanine (Restaurant) | Mezzanine / Mezzanine Bar | Restaurant area mezzanine |

**Motel Section (East/Ground Level)**
| Room / Location | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Green Bedroom | Green / Green Bedroom | Motel ground room A, objective room |
| Red Bedroom | Red / Red Bedroom | Motel ground room B, objective room |
| Motel Bathroom | Bathroom / Motel Bath | Motel area utility |
| Closet | Closet | Small utility space in motel area (rework addition) |

**Garage/Utility Section**
| Room / Location | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Mechanic Shop | Mechanic / Mechanic Shop | Garage/work area, consolidated in rework, objective room |
| Bike Repair | Bike Repair | Part of consolidated Mechanic Shop area |
| Compressor Room | Compressor | Consolidated into Mechanic Shop in rework |
| Gear Store | Gear / Gear Store | Consolidated into Mechanic Shop in rework |
| Beer Fridge | Beer Fridge | Consolidated into Mechanic Shop in rework |
| Garage Lounge | Garage Lounge / Garage | Shrunken in rework, connects to 2F Garage Lounge |
| Office Supplies | Office Supplies | Utility area with soft wall access |

**Circulation Areas**
| Room / Location | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Interior Hallway / Interior Connection | Interior / Mid | Main connecting hallway between roadhouse and motel |
| Garage Stairs | Garage Stairs | Primary vertical connection in garage area |
| Restaurant Stairs | Restaurant Stairs / Down | Primary vertical connection in restaurant area |

### 2F (Upper Floor / Motel Rooms)

| Office | Office / Office | Administrative area, objective room |

### Exterior / Roof

| Motel exterior | Motel Side / East | East-side outdoor space |
| Side areas | Side / North / South | Building flanks |
| Roof | Roof | Accessible via stairs (3rd attacker spawn location) |

---

## Key Walls & Hatches

### Critical Reinforced Walls
- **Green/Red Bedroom connecting wall (1F motel):** Rework made this entirely destructible for increased flexibility in bedroom site
- **Mechanic Shop / Kitchen connecting wall (1F):** Reinforced to compartmentalize utility objectives
- **Roadhouse / Motel interior connecting wall:** Often reinforced to limit rotation between building sections
- **Party Room / Office connecting wall (2F):** Reinforced to control 2F upper site compartmentalization
- **Office walls (2F):** Party Room to Office door removed in rework; soft walls repositioned

### Key Soft Walls
- **Hallway walls (1F roadhouse):** Multiple soft wall options enabling roamer rotation and attacker breach points
- **Shark Bar walls:** Common roamer movement paths
- **Mechanic Shop / Kitchen exterior walls:** Enable external pressure from driveway area (rework adjustment)
- **Party Room / Office soft walls (2F):** Changed from plaster to wood in rework for adjustment
- **Terrace area destructible walls (2F):** New in rework, connect Mezzanine, Piano Room, and Games Room with destructible walls to southwest
- **Restaurant stairs area soft walls:** Soft walls added above Restaurant Stairs in rework
- **Green/Red Bedroom connecting wall (1F):** Entirely destructible in rework (from previous reinforced)

### Key Hatches
- **2F to 1F hatch (Garage area):** Primary vertical connection between Garage Lounge areas; ladder also connects (rework addition)
- **2F to 1F hatch (Restaurant area):** Primary vertical connection between Restaurant/Mezzanine areas
- **2F Piano Room to Closet hatch (Motel):** New hatch in rework, replaced stairs in motel area
- **Secondary hatches:** Additional hatch options depending on room layouts (verify exact 3 hatches total per wiki)
- **Garage ladder connection:** Connects 1F Garage Lounge to 2F Garage Lounge (rework addition)

---

## Camera Locations (Default)

- **Shark Bar camera (1F):** Positioned in Shark Bar for roadhouse coverage
- **Motel camera (2F):** Positioned near Piano Room or Dorms for upper motel coverage
- **Kitchen/Mechanic camera (1F):** Positioned to monitor ground utility objectives
- **Games Room camera (2F):** Positioned in Games Room for entertainment area coverage
- **Exterior camera:** Positioned on driveway or building exterior to watch attacker approach routes
- **Valkyrie positioning:** Often placed on interior hallway overhangs, Garage/Restaurant mezzanine edges, or Shark Bar for unusual angles. Positioned to monitor roamer rotation paths and multi-floor pressure coordination.
- **Mozzie:** Typically deployed in hallways (roadhouse main hall, interior connector, motel hallway), near site entries, Kitchen/Mechanic area, Shark Bar, or exterior on driveway to watch attacker approach routes and exterior breaches

---

## Attack Strategies & Key Angles

**Two Building/Multi-Section Pressure:**
- Roadhouse (west 1F) and Motel (east 1F & 2F) are distinct attack zones — don't spread utility too thin between both
- Establish objective location early via drones (4 possible bomb sites across 2F and 1F)
- Dual-objective sites require coordinated multi-floor execution when applicable

**Exterior Pressure (Driveway/Fuel Pumps/Camping):**
- Large driveway and exterior areas offer significant external pressure opportunities
- Multiple exterior doors and windows enable flanking pressure routes
- Three attacker spawn locations provide flexible approach vectors
- Exterior roamer can create meaningful pressure without directly approaching objective

**Vertical Pressure:**
- Multiple upper floor objectives (Piano/Laundry, Party/Office) vulnerable to vertical pressure
- Identify and target hatch locations early during site execute
- Rework added ladder connection in Garage area — new vertical vector
- Vertical coordination combined with ground-level entry overwhelms defender setup

**Destructibility & Soft Wall Exploitation:**
- Map is highly destructible — use soft walls for sightline control and rotation denial
- Rework shifted soft wall placement; study new destruction patterns
- Exterior wall destruction enables unconventional breach angles
- Terrace area rework added destructible walls — new attack vectors

**Site-Specific Execution:**
- **Piano Room / Laundry (2F motel):** Vertical pressure critical; ground-level interior stairs supplementary. Motel compact layout requires precise coordination.
- **Party Room / Office (2F):** Clear adjacent hallway areas first. Rework changed wall materials (plaster to wood) — adjust destruction strategy. Multi-angle pressure on both rooms.
- **Green Bedroom / Red Bedroom (1F motel):** Ground-level motel entry from exterior side combined with interior hallway pressure. Bedroom wall now entirely destructible (rework).
- **Mechanic Shop / Kitchen (1F roadhouse):** Clear adjacent Shark Bar and circulation first. Multiple exterior doors enable multi-vector pressure. Rework consolidated garage layout — verify new destruction points.

**Key angles to watch:**
- Shark Bar corners (roamer ambush points)
- Hallway junctions (building connector crossfires)
- Interior hallway corners (connection point vulnerabilities)
- Motel hallway tight corners (narrow corridor conflicts)
- Mezzanine edges (elevated roamer positions, rework emphasis)
- Driveway approaches (exterior roamer/spawn peek positions)
- Window/door breach points (exterior entry vectors)
- Garage/Restaurant ladder areas (new vertical vulnerability, rework)

**Drone usage:**
- Scout 1F roadhouse first for roamer locations and objective site
- Reserve drone for 2F reconnaissance (Piano/Laundry or Party/Office)
- Mid-round drones for execute phase intel and hatch position confirmation

---

## Defense Strategies & Anchor Spots

**Anchor Positioning (per site):**
- **Piano Room / Laundry (2F motel):** Anchor holds one room; consolidation critical in tight motel layout. Roamer must monitor vertical hatch and ladder threats from below. Communication essential due to floor separation.
- **Party Room / Office (2F):** Anchor positioning flexible between rooms (rework wall changes affect default setup). Adjacent corridor control important. Can split defense or consolidate depending on utility availability. Roamer plays adjacent offices for flank denial.
- **Green Bedroom / Red Bedroom (1F motel):** Anchor consolidates between bedrooms. Bedroom connecting wall now entirely destructible (rework) — adjust positioning accordingly. Roamer plays motel hallway for entry denial. Interior and exterior pressure vectors both possible.
- **Mechanic Shop / Kitchen (1F roadhouse):** Anchor holds one room depending on setup. Rework consolidated garage area — verify new layout and holding positions. Roamer plays Shark Bar and adjacent hallways for Kitchen-side flank denial. Exterior pressure is primary vulnerability.

**Roamer Routes:**
- **Shark Bar roamer:** Primary 1F roamer position controlling Shark Bar area and mid-roadhouse space. Can swing to flank Kitchen or Mechanic Shop objectives.
- **Motel roamer (1F):** Ground-level motel hallway and bedroom area roamer for entry denial and lateral flanking
- **Upper motel roamer (2F):** Dedicated vertical threat monitor near hatches and ladder (rework addition) for upper site defense
- **Interior connector roamer:** Advanced roamer playing interior connecting hallway between roadhouse and motel sections (vulnerable transition point)
- **Garage area roamer:** Garage Lounge and ladder area coverage (rework emphasis); can deny vertical coordination

**Roaming Coordination:**
- Roadhouse section (west) allows flexible roaming through multiple areas (Shark Bar, hallways, mezzanines)
- Motel section is tighter; roamers have limited movement but multiple entry vectors
- Coordinate interior connector crossing — key vulnerable transition point
- Vertical threat monitoring critical for 2F sites; ladder connection creates new vulnerability (rework)
- Mezzanine monitoring important for mid-floor roamer coordination (rework emphasis on mezzanines)

---

## Stack Performance Notes (Y10S4)

| Player | Operator | Win Rate | K/D | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Sarge | — | 83.3% | — | Excellent performance, strong results |
| Grant | — | 62.5% | — | Solid positive results |
| Hound | — | 66.7% | — | Good performance, consistent |
| Smigs | — | — | — | Data unclear for Outback specifically |
| Peej | — | _TBD_ | — | New to main stack 2026-03-21; data pending |
| Slug | — | — | — | Data unclear *(B Team as of 2026-03-21; historical data)* |

**Notes:** Outback shows solid overall performance from the stack with Sarge leading at 83.3%, Grant at 62.5%, and Hound at 66.7%. All measured players show positive win rates. No strategies have been developed despite strong statistical performance — significant opportunity to formalize successful patterns into strategic frameworks. The Y7S3 rework (High Calibre) made notable layout changes to Garage and Restaurant areas; recommend re-verifying strategy effectiveness against reworked map geometry.

---

*Confidence: ✅ High — Verified via Rainbow Six Wiki, Mar 2026. All four bomb sites confirmed: (1) Piano Room / Laundry (2F), (2) Party Room / Office (2F), (3) Green Bedroom / Red Bedroom (1F), (4) Mechanic Shop / Kitchen (1F). Core room names verified from wiki including Shark Bar, Mechanical Bull, Games Room, Dorms, Motel Hallway, Garage Lounge, Office Supplies, and all primary circulation areas. High Calibre rework details confirmed: Garage consolidation of Compressor Room/Gear Store/Beer Fridge into Mechanic Shop; Green/Red Bedroom wall made entirely destructible; Piano Room hatch to Closet (replacing stairs); ladder connection between Garage levels; Terrace internalized with destructible walls to southwest. Three attacker spawns confirmed (Fuel Pumps, Storage Yard, Camping/Roof), three primary hatches plus ladder. High confidence for coaching purposes.*

overviewContent:
# Skyscraper — Overview

**Status:** ⚠️ Partial Development (Map Reference Only)
**Developed:** 2026-03-14
**Data Source:** Skyscraper.md + floor plan images

---

## Summary

Skyscraper is a compact, vertical-focused map featuring a Japanese high-rise building with rooftop dominance and window-based entry vectors. The map emphasizes exterior rooftop pressure balanced against interior anchor positions. Four bomb sites split across two floors: Tea Room/Karaoke and Exhibition/Office on 2F, Kitchen/BBQ and Bedroom/Bathroom on 1F.

**Stack Performance (Y11S1):** Skyscraper is NOT in the first-half pool; it returns at Y11S1 mid-season swap. Team shows mixed performance variance — Grant and Sarge both 60% (solid), while Hound, Slug *(B Team as of 2026-03-21)*, and Smigs struggle at ~40%. Peej data pending (new to main stack 2026-03-21). Rooftop/exterior play appears to be a skill differentiator. No developed strats exist; opportunity for structure-based preparation before mid-season return.
⚠️ **Roster change 2026-03-21:** Peej (Ash/Brava ATK, Maestro/Valkyrie DEF) replaces Slug (Zero/Jackal ATK, Smoke/Valkyrie DEF) in main stack.

---

## Pool Status

**Current Pool:** NOT in Y11S1 first-half (returns at mid-season swap)
**Return Schedule:** Expected at Y11S1 mid-season swap
**Priority:** Medium — Reasonable all-time WR (44.4% for Grant); solid opportunity for mid-season ramp-up.

---

## Bomb Sites

| Site | Rooms | Floor | Notes |
|------|-------|-------|-------|
| **Site 1** | Tea Room / Karaoke | 2F | Japanese hospitality + entertainment. Window-vulnerable site. |
| **Site 2** | Exhibition / Office | 2F | Art gallery + office. Less exterior pressure post-rework. |
| **Site 3** | Kitchen / BBQ | 1F | Food service + grilling. Moderate exterior window access. |
| **Site 4** | Bedroom / Bathroom | 1F | Residential + utility. Exterior pressure reduced post-rework. |

---

## Map Characteristics

### Vertical Structure
- **2F:** Two bomb sites (Tea/Karaoke west, Exhibition/Office east) with central Study/Storage/Hallway connecting area.
- **1F:** Two bomb sites split (Kitchen/BBQ north, Bedroom/Office south) with central Museum/Entrance circulation.
- **Rooftop:** Extensive exterior rooftop with zipline, rappel routes, and window access points.

### Key Features
- **Rooftop dominance:** Attackers can establish rooftop control early; extensive window breach points enable overwhelming vertical pressure.
- **Y5S4 rework impact:** Window removals on 2F Office, 1F Bedroom, and 1F Bathroom reduced exterior breach vulnerability. Balcony removal (west/north side) reduced runout exposure. Dragon Corridor (2F Shrine) added new narrow passage.
- **Window-heavy attack:** Many rooms have exterior windows; coordinating rooftop control with window breaches is primary attacker advantage.
- **Interior-focused defense:** Post-rework, defenders can rely more on interior anchoring; exterior pressure less omnipresent than original version.

### Why Performance Variance Exists
- **Rooftop/exterior skill differentiator:** Grant and Sarge (both 60%) likely excel at rooftop control, window sequencing, and exterior pressure management.
- **Interior anchor gaps:** Hound, Slug *(B Team)*, Smigs (~40%) suggest weaker interior anchor discipline or roamer read capability. Peej data pending.
- **Rework adaptation:** Post-Y5S4 window removal may not align with all players' exterior-pressure playstyles; some may struggle with new interior-focused meta.

---

## Stack Performance Analysis

| Player | Role | WR | Notes |
|--------|------|-----|-------|
| **Grant** | IGL / Flex | 60% | Strong rooftop/exterior reads. IGL decisions favor window-breach strats. |
| **Sarge** | Fragger | 60% | Entry fragging excels in rooftop/window scenarios. Peak performer on this map. |
| **Hound** | Anchor | ~40% | Below average. Interior anchor positioning may not align with Skyscraper anchor needs. |
| **Peej** | Flex | _TBD_ | New to main stack 2026-03-21; no prior Skyscraper data |
| **Slug** | Flex | ~40% | Below average. *(B Team as of 2026-03-21; historical data)* |
| **Smigs** | Hard Breach | ~40% | Below average. Hard breach utility may be inefficient in window-heavy execute strats. |

**Key Gap:** Grant and Sarge excel (60%) while others lag (40%). Suggests rooftop/exterior coordination and window-breach timing is differentiator. Interior breach utilities (hard breach) less valuable on this map; window/rappel entries primary.

---

## Strat Development Status

| Site | ATK | DEF | Notes |
|------|-----|-----|-------|
| **Site 1 (Tea/Karaoke)** | ❌ Not Developed | ❌ Not Developed | 2F site; rooftop-vulnerable. Window-pressure strat critical. |
| **Site 2 (Exhibition/Office)** | ❌ Not Developed | ❌ Not Developed | 2F site; reduced exterior post-rework. Less rooftop pressure. |
| **Site 3 (Kitchen/BBQ)** | ❌ Not Developed | ❌ Not Developed | 1F site; moderate window access. Sushi/Restaurant coordination critical. |
| **Site 4 (Bedroom/Bathroom)** | ❌ Not Developed | ❌ Not Developed | 1F site; window removal reduced exterior. Interior-focused execute. |

---

## Development Priorities

1. **Formalize rooftop control protocol** — Establish baseline rooftop dominance strat; who establishes rooftop, how many players, roamer management.
2. **Build primary ATK strat for Site 1 (Tea/Karaoke)** — Most window-vulnerable site; coordinate rooftop entry with interior Study clear.
3. **Study Grant/Sarge success patterns** — Analyze their rooftop and window-breach coordination; formalize into team playbook.
4. **Interior anchor discipline (Hound focus)** — Understand why 40% WR; improve interior hold positions and cross-angle cover.
5. **Window sequencing and timing** — Establish synchronized window breach timing to prevent defender repositioning.
6. **Vertical roamer denial (1F sites)** — Main Stairs control critical; formalize roamer reads for vertical stack pressure.

---

## Mid-Season Preparation Strategy

Since Skyscraper returns at mid-season swap, use this timeframe strategically:

- **Weeks 1-4 (before swap):** Develop foundational strats and run practice scrims on Skyscraper.
- **Weeks 5-8 (swap period):** Team should have solid rooftop/window strat baseline before competing.
- **Post-swap:** Leverage developed strats for competitive advantage during mid-season meta shift.

---

## Notes

- **Rooftop is attacker advantage:** If rooftop is contested, defenders lose most of their pressure denial capability. Establish rooftop dominance early.
- **Window removal impact:** Post-Y5S4, interior defending more viable than original version. Expect less reliance on rooftop defense; more interior anchor focus.
- **Grant/Sarge benchmark:** Their 60% WR suggests rooftop/exterior play is learnable skill. Use their gameplay as model for Hound/Peej/Smigs improvement.
- **Hard breach less valuable:** Traditional hard breach (Thermite/Ace) less critical on this map; window breachers (Sledge, Goyo, etc.) more flexible.

---

_Last Updated: 2026-03-14_
referenceContent:
# Skyscraper — Map Reference

**Environment:** Japanese high-rise skyscraper in Nagoya; Yakuza headquarters styled as luxury mansion with modern and classical Japanese architecture
**Floors:** Ground Floor (1F), Upper Floor (2F), Rooftop (exterior/accessible)
**In Y10S4 Ranked Pool:** Yes
**Year Added / Reworked:** Added Y2S1 (Operation Red Crow, 2017); reworked Y5S4 (Operation Neon Dawn, 2020)

---

## Overview

Skyscraper is a compact, vertical-focused map featuring two distinct buildings—a house and a restaurant—connected by narrow hallways on two indoor floors (1F, 2F) with accessible rooftop. The map emphasizes window play and rappelling; extensive exterior balconies and windows enable rooftop attack vectors. The Y5S4 Neon Dawn rework removed many balconies (especially west and north 2F sides) to reduce runout and spawnpeak vulnerability, and added Dragon (2F Shrine) with updated exterior pressure zones. Heavy exterior window pressure and interior narrow hallways create unique tactical dynamics. Attackers can exploit rooftop control and window breaches; defenders must actively manage exterior pressure while navigating tight interior corridors. Stack shows mixed performance variance (Grant/Sarge 60% vs Hound/Peej/Smigs ~40% (Slug B Team as of 2026-03-21)), suggesting rooftop/exterior play is a skill differentiator.

---

## Bomb Sites

### Site 1: Tea Room / Karaoke — 2F
**Rooms:** Tea Room (Japanese hospitality area) and Karaoke (entertainment/event venue)
**Description:** Upper floor site with hospitality/entertainment aesthetic. Tea Room is distinguished Japanese-styled space; Karaoke is large entertainment area. Multiple exterior windows on Tea Room side create window breach vulnerability. Connected via interior hallways.
**Common defense:** Anchor holds site with flexibility between Tea and Karaoke depending on setup. Roamer manages exterior window pressure from rooftop area. Communication critical due to floor separation.
**Attack approach:** Establish rooftop window control. Coordinate window breaches with interior pressure on both rooms. Clear hallway connections first to deny roamer flanks. Exterior rooftop pressure is primary advantage.

### Site 2: Exhibition Room / Office — 2F
**Rooms:** Exhibition Room (art/display space, formerly called Office per wiki features) and Office (administrative area)
**Description:** Upper floor site with cultural/administrative aesthetic. Exhibition Room provides spacious gallery space; Office is administrative work area. Window removal in rework (Y5S4) reduced exterior pressure vulnerability compared to original. Good interior rotation flexibility.
**Common defense:** Anchor positioning flexible between rooms. Window reduction (rework) makes interior defense more viable. Roamer plays hallways and adjacent areas (Study, Storage) for coordination and flank denial.
**Attack approach:** Interior clear priority after rework window removal. Clear Study/Storage areas first to deny roamer flanks. Multi-angle pressure on both rooms. Exterior window pressure less critical than original version.

### Site 3: Kitchen / BBQ — 1F
**Rooms:** Kitchen (food preparation area) and BBQ (grilling/dining area)
**Description:** Ground floor site with culinary aesthetic. Kitchen is food prep space; BBQ is grilling/social dining area. Rework adjusted Kitchen size and layout; BBQ area offers moderate exterior window access. Interior layout provides rotation flexibility.
**Common defense:** Anchor holds Kitchen or BBQ depending on setup. Roamer plays adjacent areas (Sushi Restaurant, Museum, Hallways) for flank denial. Interior-focused defense; window pressure moderate.
**Attack approach:** Clear adjacent Sushi Restaurant and Museum areas first. Coordinate multi-room breach on Kitchen/BBQ objectives. Interior clear priority; exterior window pressure supplementary due to rework adjustments.

### Site 4: Bedroom / Bathroom — 1F
**Rooms:** Bedroom (residential suite) and Bathroom (utility space)
**Description:** Ground floor site with residential aesthetic. Bedroom is expanded in rework (larger than original) for less claustrophobic feel. Bathroom is utility space. Window removal in rework (Y5S4) significantly reduced external breach vulnerability. Interior-focused objective.
**Common defense:** Anchor consolidates in Bedroom or Bathroom depending on space control preference. Rework window removal makes interior anchoring more viable. Roamer plays adjacent areas and hallways for flank denial.
**Attack approach:** Interior breach focus due to window removal in rework. Clear adjacent hallway and connecting areas first. Multi-angle pressure on both rooms. Less vertical window pressure than original version.

---

## Room Names & Community Callouts

### 2F (Upper Floor)
| Room / Location | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Master Bedroom | Bedroom / Master | Master suite, luxury apartment area |
| Dressing Room | Dressing / Dressing Room | Wardrobe/clothing area |
| Karaoke Bar | Karaoke / Karaoke Bar | Entertainment venue, distinctive space |
| Dragon Corridor | Dragon / Dragon Hallway | Japanese-themed passage |
| Tea Room | Tea / Tea Room | Japanese hospitality room |
| Study | Study | Office/study area |
| Storage | Storage | Equipment/supply storage |
| Hallway (main) | Hallway / Hall | Primary 2F circulation |
| Bathroom | Bathroom | Utility room |
| Stairs | Stairs / Main Stairs | Primary vertical access |

### 1F (Ground Floor)
| Room / Location | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Sushi Restaurant | Sushi / Restaurant | Dining area with kitchen counter |
| Kitchen | Kitchen / Kit | Food preparation area |
| Museum | Museum / Art | Art display/gallery area |
| Office Lounge | Office / Office Lounge / Lounge | Office break room |
| Bathroom | Bathroom | Utility room |
| Hallway (main) | Hallway / Hall | Primary 1F circulation |
| Garden Room | Garden / Patio | Indoor garden/plant area |
| Stairs | Stairs | Vertical access to 2F |

### Rooftop & Exterior
| Location | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Rooftop (general) | Roof / Rooftop | Main roof area |
| East side rooftop | East Roof / East | Building east exterior roof |
| West side rooftop | West Roof / West | Building west exterior roof |
| North area | North / North Roof | Building north rooftop |
| South area | South / South Roof | Building south rooftop |
| Zipline (east-west connection) | Zipline / Zip | Zipline connecting building sides |
| Rappel points | Rappel / Drop | Window rappel access points |
| Exterior windows (2F) | Windows / Outside | Extensive window exterior approaches |
| Exterior windows (1F) | Windows / Outside | Ground floor exterior window access |

---

## Key Walls & Hatches

### Critical Reinforced Walls
- **Bedroom / Dressing connecting wall (2F):** Typically reinforced to separate site areas; controls interior sight lines
- **Karaoke / Dragon connecting wall (2F):** Reinforced to compartmentalize site objectives
- **Sushi / Kitchen connecting wall (1F):** Reinforced to separate restaurant and prep areas
- **Museum / Office connecting wall (1F):** Reinforced to create distinct site compartments
- **Tea Room walls (2F):** Reinforced for interior tactical advantage

### Key Soft Walls
- **Hallway walls (all floors):** Multiple soft wall options for roamer rotation and attacker breach points
- **Dragon Corridor walls:** Soft wall options in narrow corridor for alternative angles
- **Garden Room walls (1F):** Soft walls enable pressure from exterior garden area
- **Study/Storage walls (2F):** Soft walls create rotation flexibility

### Key Hatches
- **2F to 1F hatch (main stairwell):** Primary vertical access point; enables vertical pressure for lower sites
- **Secondary hatches:** May exist depending on interior layout ⚠️ Verify exact hatch positioning
- **Rooftop access points:** Zipline/rappel routes are primary rooftop access (not traditional hatches)

---

## Camera Locations (Default)

- **2F objective camera:** Positioned in Bedroom or Karaoke area for upper site coverage
- **1F objective camera:** Positioned in Sushi or Museum area for ground floor monitoring
- **Hallway camera:** Interior circulation monitoring
- **Rooftop camera:** Exterior camera monitoring roof approach (if applicable)
- **Valkyrie positioning:** Often placed on window ledges (unusual, hard to destroy), rooftop overhangs, or interior hallway overhangs for vertical angle coverage. Placed to monitor zipline approaches, window breach points, and interior rotations.
- **Mozzie:** Typically deployed in hallways (main 1F hall, 2F corridor), near site entries, on rooftop perimeter, or exterior windows to watch attacker approach routes and window/zipline breach coordination

---

## Attack Strategies & Key Angles

**Rooftop Control (Primary Advantage):**
- Establish rooftop dominance early — rooftop is high-ground advantage for attackers
- Identify zipline route and use for flanking pressure
- Multiple window breaches from rooftop create overwhelming vertical pressure on defenders
- Rappel routes into objective windows are high-value breach points

**Window Breach Coordination:**
- Nearly every room has exterior windows — establish exterior control early
- Coordinate simultaneous window breaches with interior pressure to split defender responses
- Window angles from rooftop/ziplines create unique entry vectors

**Multi-Floor Execution:**
- If attacking multiple sites across floors, establish floor dominance first
- 2F sites are more vulnerable to rooftop pressure; 1F sites more vulnerable to interior/garden pressure
- Vertical access via main stairwell is supplementary to exterior rooftop approaches

**Site-Specific Approaches:**
- **Bedroom/Dressing:** Heavy rooftop pressure with coordinated window breaches; interior entry supplementary
- **Karaoke/Dragon:** Rooftop window control + zipline flanking; Dragon Corridor narrow approach requires precision
- **Sushi/Kitchen:** Coordinate rooftop pressure above with interior multi-room clear below
- **Museum/Office:** Interior clear + garden area exterior pressure + window management

**Key angles to watch:**
- Rooftop zipline corners (roamer counter-ambush)
- Window breach points (defender counter-fire from interior)
- Hallway junctions (roamer ambush, vertical control)
- Stairwell landing (defender holding vertical control)
- Garden area corners (exterior roamer positions)
- Tea Room (secondary 2F holding position)

**Drone usage:**
- Scout rooftop immediately to establish exterior threat assessment
- 2nd drone for interior recon and roamer locations
- Reserve mid-round drones for execute phase

---

## Defense Strategies & Anchor Spots

**Anchor Positioning (per site):**
- **Bedroom/Dressing (2F):** Anchor holds interior position (Bedroom preferred for space). Must rely on roomer/exterior defender to manage heavy window pressure from rooftop. Cannot hold both interior and exterior alone.
- **Karaoke/Dragon (2F):** Anchor holds site, often Dragon Corridor for tight angles. Roamer manages rooftop/window exterior pressure. Dragon's narrow corridor offers strong defensive position.
- **Sushi/Kitchen (1F):** Anchor holds Kitchen for utility control. Roamer plays adjacent areas (Museum, Garden) for flank denial. Less rooftop pressure than 2F but window management still critical.
- **Museum/Office (1F):** Anchor holds one room; flexibility between Museum/Office depending on defender commitment. Roamer plays Garden area and hallways for flanking denial.

**Roamer Routes:**
- **Rooftop roamer:** CRITICAL ROLE — manages rooftop area, denies zipline usage, counters window breaches from exterior. High-skill positioning managing rappel/zipline threats.
- **Upper roamer (2F):** Plays 2F hallways, adjacent rooms (Tea, Study, Storage), manages interior corridor pressure and secondary objective support
- **Lower roamer (1F):** Plays 1F hallways, Garden area, Museum/Office peripheral zones. Can rotate to Garden for exterior denial.
- **Vertical roamer:** Manages stairwell area, denies interior vertical pressure, provides mid-floor impact

**Exterior Pressure Management:**
- Rooftop/zipline pressure is primary threat — never leave rooftop uncontested
- Window pressure must be managed actively by roamer and possibly anchor sacrifice
- Interior defenders can hold tight positions but must accept exterior vulnerability
- Communication between roomer and anchor critical for coordinated window defense

---

## Stack Performance Notes (Y10S4)

| Player | Operator | Win Rate | K/D | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Grant | — | 60% | 1.71 | Solid performance, consistent |
| Sarge | — | 60% | — | Equivalent performance to Grant |
| Hound | — | ~40% | — | Weaker results, below average |
| Peej | — | _TBD_ | — | New to main stack 2026-03-21; data pending |
| Slug | — | ~40% | — | Weaker results *(B Team as of 2026-03-21; historical data)* |
| Smigs | — | ~40% | — | Weaker results, below average |

**Notes:** Skyscraper shows significant performance variance within the stack. Grant and Sarge both at 60% while Hound, Peej, and Smigs struggle at ~40% (Slug B Team as of 2026-03-21; historical data shown). This suggests rooftop play and exterior window management may be skill-dependent. The gap between strong (60%) and weak (40%) performers is substantial, indicating specific playstyle or positioning knowledge differences. No strategies developed despite mixed results; opportunity to analyze Grant/Sarge success patterns versus struggling performers' approach. Rooftop exterior play may be the differentiator.

---

*Confidence: ✅ High — Verified via Rainbow Six Wiki, Mar 2026. All four bomb sites confirmed from wiki rework features: (1) Tea Room / Karaoke (2F), (2) Exhibition Room / Office (2F), (3) Kitchen / BBQ (1F), (4) Bedroom / Bathroom (1F). Y5S4 Neon Dawn rework changes documented: 2F Shrine (Dragon) added, west/north balconies removed, window removals in 2F Office, 1F Bedroom, 1F Bathroom; 1F Bathroom and Bedroom expanded; 1F Delivery adjusted to accommodate House site. Core room names verified (Tea Room, Karaoke, Exhibition, Office, Kitchen, BBQ, Bedroom, Bathroom, Study, Storage, Hallways). Two hatches confirmed. Three attacker spawns (Helipad, Tower, Ventilation Units). High confidence for coaching purposes.*
